entries.name: Optional Class Feature Selection
entries: Class features in this section are not automatically gained. Consult your DM to select features if you meet the level requirement stated in the feature's description.
Features can be selected separately; you may choose one, multiple, or none of them.
Features replace existing class features when selected. If a feature replaces another, you lose all benefits from the replaced feature and no longer qualify for anything requiring it.

entries.name: Replacing Features
entries: Selecting a feature that replaces another removes all benefits from the replaced feature.
You lose资格 for any game effects that require the replaced feature.

entries.name: Barbarian Skill Proficiency
entries: At 3rd level and again at 10th level, you gain proficiency in one skill of your choice from the list of skills available to barbarians at 1st level.

entries.name: 7th-Level Feature
entries: As part of the bonus action you take to enter your rage, you can move up to half your speed.

entries.name: Primal Path Feature
entries: At 3rd level, a barbarian gains the Primal Path feature, which offers you the choice of a subclass.
Available subclasses: {@item Path of the Beast|TCE} and {@item Path of Wild Magic|TCE}

entries.name: Path of the Beast
entries: Barbarians who walk the Path of the Beast draw their rage from a bestial spark burning within their souls. That beast bursts forth in the throes of rage, physically transforming the barbarian.
You can choose the origin of your feral might or determine it by rolling on the Origin of the Beast table.

entries.name: Lycanthrope Heritage
entries: One of your parents is a lycanthrope, and you've inherited some of their curse.

entries.name: Archdruid Descent
entries: You are descended from an archdruid and inherited the ability to partially change shape.

entries.name: Fey Bestial Gift
entries: A fey spirit gifted you with the ability to adopt different bestial aspects.
An ancient animal spirit dwells within you, allowing you to walk this path.

entries.name: Path of the Beast (3rd Level)
entries: When you enter your rage, you can transform, revealing the bestial power within you. Until the rage ends, you manifest a natural weapon. It counts as a simple melee weapon for you, and you add your Strength modifier to the attack and damage rolls when you attack with it, as normal.

entries.name: Weapon Form Choices
items: Bite: Your mouth transforms into a bestial muzzle or great mandibles (your choice). It deals 1d8 piercing damage on a hit. Once on each of your turns when you damage a creature with this bite, you regain a number of hit points equal to your proficiency bonus, provided you have less than half your hit points when you hit.

Claws: Each of your hands transforms into a claw, which you can use as a weapon if it's empty. It deals 1d6 slashing damage on a hit. Once on each of your turns when you attack with a claw using the Attack action, you can make one additional claw attack as part of the same action.

Tail: You grow a lashing, spiny tail, which deals 1d8 piercing damage on a hit and has the reach property. If a creature you can see within 10 feet of you hits you with an attack roll, you can use your reaction to swipe your tail and roll a d8, applying a bonus to your AC equal to the number rolled, potentially causing the attack to miss you.

entries.name: Natural Weapons as Magical
entries: The feral power within you increases, causing the natural weapons of your Form of the Beast to count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.

entries.name: Rest Benefit
entries: When you finish a short or long rest, choose one of the following benefits, which lasts until you finish your next short or long rest:

items: You gain a swimming speed equal to your walking speed, and you can breathe underwater.
You gain a climbing speed equal to your walking speed, and you can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.
When you jump, you can make a Strength (Athletics) check and extend your jump by a number of feet equal to the check's total. You can make this special check only once per turn.

entries.name: Infected Fury
entries: When you hit a creature with your natural weapons while raging, you can curse the target with rabid fury. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC 8 + Constitution modifier + proficiency bonus) or suffer one of the following effects (your choice):

items: The target must use its reaction to make a melee attack against another creature of your choice that you can see.

entries: You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Call the Hunt
entries: When you enter your rage, choose a number of willing creatures you can see within 30 feet equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum 1). Each chosen creature grants you 5 temporary hit points.
Until the rage ends, chosen creatures can use the following benefit once per turn: on a hit with an attack, roll a d6 and add the number rolled to damage dealt.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining all uses on a long rest.

entries.name: Mechanics
items: Temporary hit points: {@temporary hit points 5 per creature}
Damage bonus: {@damage roll a d6 and add result to damage}
Usage limit: {@uses per day proficiency bonus}, {@recover long rest}

entries.name: Path of Wild Magic Overview
entries: TASHA Many places in the multiverse abound with beauty, intense emotion, and rampant magic; the Feywild, the Upper Planes, and other realms of supernatural power radiate with such forces and can profoundly influence people. As folk of deep feeling, barbarians are especially susceptible to these wild influences, with some barbar, being transformed by the magic. These magic-suffused barbarians walk the Path of Wild Magic. Elf, tiefling, aasimar, and genasi barbarians often seek this path, eager to manifest the otherworldly magic of their ancestors.

entries.name: Magic Awareness
entries.description: As an action, you can open your awareness to the presence of concentrated magic. Until the end of your next turn, you know the location of any spell or magic item within 60 feet of you that isn't behind total cover. When you sense a spell, you learn which school of magic it belongs to.

entries.name: Wild Magic Effect
entries: When you enter your rage, roll on the Wild Magic table to determine the magical effect produced.

entries.name: Saving Throw DC
entries: If the effect requires a saving throw, the DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution modifier.

Shadowy tendrils lash around you. Each creature of your choice that you can see within 30 feet of you must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take {@damage 1d8} necrotic damage. You also gain {@damage 1d8} temporary hit points.
You teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. Until your rage ends, you can use this effect again on each of your turns as a bonus action.

An intangible spirit, which looks like a flumph or a pixie (your choice), appears within 5 feet of one creature of your choice that you can see within 30 feet of you. At the end of the current turn, the spirit explodes, and each creature within 5 feet of it must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 1d6} force damage. Until your rage ends, you can use this effect again, summoning another spirit, on each of your turns as a bonus action.

Magic infuses one weapon of your choice that you are holding. Until your rage ends, the weapon's damage type changes to force, and it gains the light and thrown properties, with a normal range of 20 feet and a long range of 60 feet. If the weapon leaves your hand, the weapon reappears in your hand at the end of the current turn.
Whenever a creature hits you with an attack roll before your rage ends, that creature takes {@damage 1d6} force damage, as magic lashes out in retribution.
You are surrounded by multicolored, protective lights; you gain a +1 bonus to AC, and while within 10 feet of you, your allies gain the same bonus.
Flowers and vines temporarily grow around you; until your rage ends, the ground within 15 feet of you is difficult terrain for your enemies.

A bolt of light shoots from your chest. Another creature of your choice that you can see within 30 feet of you must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take {@damage 1d6} radiant damage and be blinded until the start of your next turn. Until your rage ends, you can use this effect again on each of your turns as a bonus action.

entries.name: Wild Magic Surge
entries.text: As an action, you can touch one creature (which can be yourself) and confer one of the following benefits of your choice to that creature: {@dice 1d3} when making an attack roll or ability check, adding the number rolled to the d20 roll; or regain one expended spell slot (level equals the number rolled or lower).

entries.name: Spell Slot Recovery
entries.text: Once a creature receives this benefit, it can't receive it again until after a long rest. You can take this action a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Imperiled During Rage
entries: When you are imperiled during your rage, the magic within you can lash out; immediately after you take damage or fail a saving throw while raging, you can use your reaction to roll on the Wild Magic table and immediately produce the effect rolled. This effect replaces your current Wild Magic effect.

items: Trigger: Taking damage or failing a saving throw while raging
Action: Use reaction to roll on {@table tasha.table.wild-magic-table} and produce the effect rolled
Effect: Replaces current Wild Magic effect

entries.name: Wild Magic at 14th Level
entries.description: Whenever you roll on the Wild Magic table, you can roll the die twice and choose which of the two effects to unleash. If you roll the same number on both dice, you can ignore the number and choose any effect on the table.

entries.name: Expanded Bard Spell List
entries: The following spells expand the bard spell list in the Player's Handbook. Spells are organized by spell level, not character level.
If a spell can be cast as a ritual, the ritual tag appears after the spell's name.
Each spell is in the Player's Handbook unless marked with an asterisk (spells in chapter 3). Xanathar's Guide to Everything also offers additional spells.

items: 3RD LEVEL: Intellect Fortress, Mass Healing Word, Slow
7TH LEVEL: Dream of the Blue Veil, Prismatic Spray

items.text: Replace one of the skills you chose for the {@feature tasha.class.bard-expertise} feature with one of your other skill proficiencies that isn't benefiting from Expertise.
Replace one cantrip you learned from this class's {@feature tasha.class.bard-spellcasting} feature with another cantrip from the bard spell list.

entries.name: Bard College Feature
entries: At 3rd level, a bard gains the Bard College feature, which offers you the choice of a subclass. The following options are available to you when making that choice: College of Creation and College of Eloquence.

entries.content: Bards believe the cosmos is a work of art—the creation of the first dragons and gods. This creative work included harmonies that continue to resound through existence today, a power known as the Song of Creation. The bards of the College of Creation draw on this primeval song through dance, music, and poetry.

Teachers of the College of Creation share this lesson: "Before the sun and the moon, there was the Song, and its music awoke the first dawn. Its melodies so delighted the stones and trees that some of them gained a voice of their own. And now they sing too. Learn the Song, students, and you too can teach the mountains to sing and dance."

Dwarves and gnomes often encourage their bards to become students of the Song of Creation. Among dragonborn, the Song of Creation is revered, for legends portray {@item Bahamut|tasha's-cauldron-of-everything.monster.bahamut} and {@item Tiamat|tasha's-cauldron-of-everything.monster.tiamat}—the greatest of dragons—as two of the song's first singers.

entries.name: Song of Creation
entries: When you give a creature a Bardic Inspiration die, you can create a Tiny mote of potential that orbits within 5 feet of the creature. The mote is intangible, invulnerable, and lasts until the Bardic Inspiration die is lost. It appears as a musical note, star, flower, or another symbol of art or life you choose.

entries.name: Effect Based on Use
entries: When the creature uses the Bardic Inspiration die for an ability check: The creature can roll the die again and choose which roll to use. The mote emits harmless sparks.

When the creature uses the Bardic Inspiration die for an attack roll: The mote shatters, dealing thunder damage equal to the number rolled on the Bardic Inspiration die to the target and creatures within 5 feet that you can see. Targets must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC or take damage.

When the creature uses the Bardic Inspiration die for a saving throw: The mote vanishes, granting the creature temporary hit points equal to the die roll plus your Charisma modifier (minimum 1 temporary hit point).

entries.name: Dancing Item
entries: Large or smaller construct
Armor Class 16 (natural armor)
Hit Points 10 + five times your bard level
Speed 30 ft., fly 30 ft. (hover)

entries.name: Condition Immunities
entries: charmed
exhaustion
poisoned
frightened

entries.name: Immutable Form
entries.description: The item is immune to any spell or effect that would alter its form.

entries.name: Irrepressible Dance
entries.description: When any creature starts its turn within 10 feet of the item, the item can increase or decrease (your choice) the walking speed of that creature by 10 feet until the end of the turn, provided the item isn't incapacitated.

entries.name: Animate Item
entries.description: As an action, you can target a Large or smaller nonmagical item you can see within 30 feet and animate it. The animated item uses the Dancing Item stat block, which uses your proficiency bonus (PB). The item is friendly to you and your companions and obeys your commands. It lives for 1 hour, until it is reduced to 0 hit points, or until you die.

entries.name: Combat Mechanics
entries.description: In combat, the item shares your initiative count but takes its turn immediately after yours. It can move and use its reaction on its own, but the only action it takes on its turn is the Dodge action, unless you take a bonus action on your turn to command it to take another action. That action can be one in its stat block or some other action. If you are incapacitated, the item can take any action of its choice, not just Dodge.

entries.name: Command via Bardic Inspiration
entries.description: When you use your Bardic Inspiration feature, you can command the item as part of the same bonus action you use for Bardic Inspiration.

entries.name: Restrictions
entries.description: Once you animate an item with this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest, unless you expend a spell slot of 3rd level or higher to use this feature again. You can have only one item animated by this feature at a time; if you use this action and already have a dancing item from this feature, the first one immediately becomes inanimate.

entries.name: Multiple Item Creation
entries.value: When using Performance of Creation, you can create multiple items at once. The number of items created equals your Charisma modifier (minimum of two items).{@modifier Charisma}

entries.name: Disappearing Item Choice
entries.value: If creating an item that would exceed the number allowed, you choose which previously created item disappears.

entries.name: Size Restrictions
entries.value: Only one item can be of the maximum size you can create; the rest must be {@size Small} or {@size Tiny}.

entries.name: No GP Value Limit
entries.value: You are no longer limited by gp value when creating items with Performance of Creation.

entries.name: College of Eloquence
entries: Adherents of the College of Eloquence master the art of oratory. These bards wield a blend of logic and theatrical wordplay, winning over skeptics and detractors with logical arguments and plucking at heartstrings to appeal to the emotions of audiences.

items: Focus on persuasive speech and rhetorical skill
Combine logical reasoning with emotional appeal
Specialize in influencing others through discourse

entries.name: Silver Tongue
entries: You are a master at saying the right thing at the right time. When you make a Charisma (Persuasion) or Charisma (Deception) check, you can treat a d20 roll of 9 or lower as a 10.

entries.name: Unsettling Words
entries.description: As a bonus action, you can expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration and choose one creature you can see within 60 feet of you. Roll the Bardic Inspiration die. The creature must subtract the number rolled from the next saving throw it makes before the start of your next turn.
entries.usage: once per use of Bardic Inspiration
effect: The creature subtracts the rolled number from its next saving throw before the start of your next turn

entries.name: 6th-level College of Eloquence Feature
entries: When a creature adds one of your Bardic Inspiration dice to its ability check, attack roll, or saving throw and the roll fails, the creature can keep the Bardic Inspiration die.

content.text: You gain the ability to make your speech intelligible to any creature. As an {@action}, choose one or more creatures within {@range 60 feet} of you, up to a number equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one creature). The chosen creatures can magically understand you, regardless of the language you speak, for {@duration 1 hour}.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest, unless you expend a {@spell-slot} to use it again.

entries.name: Encouraging Others
entries.description: When you successfully inspire someone, the power of your eloquence can now spread to someone else. When a creature within 60 feet of you adds one of your Bardic Inspiration dice to its ability check, attack roll, or saving throw and the roll succeeds, you can use your reaction to encourage a different creature (other than yourself) that can hear you within 60 feet of you, giving it a Bardic Inspiration die without expending any of your Bardic Inspiration uses.

items: number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once)

entries.name: New Features and Subclasses
entries.description: The cleric class receives new features and subclasses in this section.

entries.name: 3RD LEVEL
entries: Aura of Vitality
Elemental Weapon
Revivify {@item PHB.Revivify}
Summon Fey {@item PHB.Summon Fey}
Intellect Fortress
Spirit Shroud
Summon Fey {@item PHB.Summon Fey}
Summon Shadowspawn {@item PHB.Summon Shadowspawn}
Summon Undead {@item PHB.Summon Undead}

entries.name: 4TH LEVEL
entries: Summon Aberration {@item PHB.Summon Aberration}

entries.name: 5TH LEVEL
entries: Mislead
Planar Binding {@item PHB.Planar Binding}
Teleportation Circle {@item PHB.Teleportation Circle}

entries.name: 2nd-level cleric feature
entries.description: As a bonus action, you touch your holy symbol, utter a prayer, and regain one expended spell slot. {@action bonus action} {@item Holy Symbol|player-companion.item.holy-symbol} {@class_feature Channel Divinity|tasha.class.cleric-channel-divinity}

entries.name: 6TH LEVEL
entries: Sunbeam {@spell 6th-level}
Flesh to Stone {@spell 6th-level}
Otiluke's Freezing Sphere {@spell 6th-level}
Tasha's Otherworldly Guise {@spell 6th-level}
Dream of the Blue Veil {@spell 6th-level}

entries.name: 8TH LEVEL
entries: Demi-Plane {@spell 8th-level}

entries.name: 9TH LEVEL
entries: Blade of Disaster {@spell 9th-level}
Gate {@spell 9th-level}
Weird {@spell 9th-level}

entries.name: Sunburst
entries: At 9th level, you can cast Power Word Heal at a higher level than half your proficiency bonus (rounded up).
The number of times you can use this feature is based on the level you've reached in this class: 2nd level, once; 6th level, twice; and 18th level, thrice.
You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: 4th-Level Cleric Feature
entries: When you reach 4th level, you can replace one cantrip you learned from this class's Spellcasting feature with another cantrip from the cleric spell list.
This ability is triggered when you gain an Ability Score Improvement (ASI) at this level.

entries.name: Blessed Strikes
entries: You are blessed with divine might in battle. When a creature takes damage from one of your cantrips or weapon attacks, you can also deal {@damage 1d8} radiant damage to that creature. Once you deal this damage, you can't use this feature again until the start of your next turn.

items: Replaces: Divine Strike or Potent Spellcasting (Cleric)

entries.name: Divine Domain Feature
entries.description: At 1st level, a cleric gains the Divine Domain feature, which offers the choice of a subclass. The following options are available to you when making that choice: Order Domain, Peace Domain, and Twilight Domain.

entries.name: Order Domain
entries.description: A subclass option for clerics, granting abilities related to divine order and law.

entries.name: Peace Domain
entries.description: A subclass option for clerics, granting abilities focused on healing and pacification.

entries.name: Twilight Domain
entries.description: A subclass option for clerics, granting abilities tied to twilight, dusk, and the liminal space between life and death.

entries.name: Description
entries: The Order Domain represents discipline, as well as devotion to the laws that govern a society, an institution, or a philosophy. Clerics of Order meditate on logic and justice as they serve their gods, examples of which appear in the Order Deities table.

entries.name: Beliefs
entries: Clerics of Order believe that well-crafted laws establish legitimate hierarchies, and those selected by law to lead must be obeyed. Those who obey must do so to the best of their ability, and if those who lead fail to protect the law, they must be replaced. In this manner, law weaves a web of obligations that create order and security in a chaotic multiverse.

entries.name: Order Domain
entries: Gain domain spells at 1st level (see Order Domain Spells table).
Domain spells function per the Divine Domain class feature from {@item Player's Handbook|PHB}.

entries.name: Twilight Domain
entries: Gain domain spells at 1st level (see Twilight Domain Spells table).
Domain spells function per the Divine Domain class feature from {@item Player's Handbook|PHB}.

entries.name: ORDER DOMAIN SPELLS
entries: The source text provided is incomplete and does not contain explicit information about the Order Domain spells. The snippet appears to reference a section about Dragonborn Clerics of Order, but lacks detailed spell lists or rules related to the Order Domain.

entries.name: Order Domain Feature
entries: You gain proficiency with heavy armor. You also gain proficiency in the Intimidation or Persuasion skill (your choice).

entries.name: Twilight Domain Feature
entries: You gain proficiency with martial weapons and heavy armor.

entries.name: Sidekick Proficiencies
items: Proficiency in one saving throw of your choice: Strength, Dexterity, or Constitution.
Proficiency in two skills of your choice from the following list: Acrobatics, Animal Handling, Athletics, Intimidation, Nature, Perception, and Survival.
Proficiency with all armor. If the sidekick is a humanoid or has a simple or martial weapon in its stat block, it gains proficiency with shields and all simple and martial weapons.

entries.name: Law's Encouragement
entries: You can invoke the power of law to embolden an ally to attack. If you cast a spell with a spell slot of 1st level or higher and target an ally with the spell, that ally can use their reaction immediately after the spell to make one weapon attack against a creature of your choice that you can see.
If the spell targets more than one ally, you choose the ally who can make the attack.

entries.name: Channel Divinity: Order's Demand
entries.description: As an action, you present your holy symbol. Each creature of your choice that can see or hear you within 30 feet of you must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by you until the end of your next turn or until the charmed creature takes any damage. You can also cause any of the charmed creatures to drop what they are holding when they fail the saving throw.

entries.name: Em bodiment of the Law
entries.description: You have become remarkably adept at channeling magical energy to compel others. If you cast a spell of the enchantment school using a spell slot of 1st level or higher, you can change the spell's casting time to 1 bonus action for this casting, provided the spell's casting time is normally 1 action. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses of it when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: 17th-Level Order Domain Feature
entries: When you deal Divine Strike damage to a creature on your turn, you can curse that creature until the start of your next turn.
The next time one of your allies hits the cursed creature with an attack, the target also takes {@damage 2d8 psychic} damage, and the curse ends.
You can curse a creature in this way only once per turn.

entries.name: Peace Domain
entries: The Peace Domain clerics inspire individuals and communities to resolve conflict, stand against forces that hinder peace, and foster harmony through their divine guidance.
Clerics of this domain preside over treaty signings and arbitrate disputes, using their blessings to unite people and share burdens. Their magic supports those who fight for peaceful resolutions.
The domain's philosophy centers on the 'balm of peace,' thriving in healthy communities, between nations, and within kindhearted souls.

items: Blessings that unite people and share burdens
Magic that aids advocates of peaceful conflict resolution

entries.text: You gain domain spells at the cleric levels listed in the {@table tasha.class.peace-domain-spells-table} table. See the {@feature phb14.class.divine-domain} class feature for how domain spells work.

entries.name: Emboldening Bond
entries: You can forge an empowering bond among people who are at peace with one another. As an action, you choose a number of willing creatures within 30 feet of you (this can include yourself) equal to your proficiency bonus. You create a magical bond among them for 10 minutes or until you use this feature again.

items: While any bonded creature is within 30 feet of another, the creature can roll a d4 and add the number rolled to an attack roll, an ability check, or a saving throw it makes.
Each creature can add the d4 no more than once per turn.

entries: You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Balm of Peace
entries: You can use your Channel Divinity to make your very presence a soothing balm. As an action, you can move up to your speed, without provoking opportunity attacks, and when you move within 5 feet of any other creature during this action, you can restore a number of hit points to that creature equal to 2d6 + your Wisdom modifier (minimum of 1 hit point). A creature can receive this healing only once whenever you take this action.

items: Restriction: Once per action, per target

entries.name: Protective Bond
entries: When a creature affected by your {@feature emboldening-bond} is about to take damage, a second bonded creature within 30 feet of the first can use its reaction to teleport to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the first creature. The second creature then takes all the damage instead.

entries.name: Cross-Reference
entries: See also: {@feature emboldening-bond} ( {@class paladin}.{@domain peace}.{@feature emboldening-bond} )

entries.name: POTENT SPELLCASTING
entries.description: You add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any cleric cantrip.

entries.name: Peace Domain 17th-Level Feature
description: The benefits of your Emboldening Bond and Protective Bond features now work when the creatures are within 60 feet of each other.
Moreover, when a creature uses Protective Bond to take someone else's damage, the creature has resistance to that damage.

entries.name: Description
entries: The twilight transition from light into darkness often brings calm and even joy, as the day's labors end and the hours of rest begin. The darkness can also bring terrors, but the gods of twilight guard against the horrors of the night.

entries.name: Role
entries: Clerics who serve these deities bring comfort to those seeking rest and protect them by venturing into the encroaching darkness to ensure that the dark is a comfort, not a terror.

entries.name: Eyes of Night
entries: You can see through the deepest gloom. You have {@darkvision 300 feet}. In that radius, you can see in dim light as if it were bright light and in darkness as if it were dim light.

entries.name: Sharing Darkvision
items: As an action, you can magically share your darkvision with willing creatures you can see within {@range 10 feet} of you.
The number of creatures you can share it with equals your {@mod Wisdom} (minimum of one creature).
The shared darkvision lasts for {@duration 1 hour}.
Once you share it, you can't do so again until you finish a {@condition long rest}, unless you expend a {@spell-slot} to share it again.

entries.name: Multiclassing
entries: You don't gain the ability to share your darkvision with other creatures unless you have a spellcasting class that allows you to expend spell slots.

entries.name: Twilight Domain 1st Level
entries: As an action, you give one creature you touch (including possibly yourself) advantage on the next initiative roll the creature makes.
This benefit ends immediately after the roll or if you use this feature again.

items: Action: Use an action to grant advantage on a creature's next initiative roll
Target: One creature you touch (self included)
Effect: Advantage on the next initiative roll
Duration: Until the initiative roll is made

entries.name: Channel Divinity: Twilight Sanctuary
entries: You can use your Channel Divinity to refresh your allies with soothing twilight.

As an action, you present your holy symbol, and a sphere of twilight emanates from you. The sphere is centered on you, has a 30-foot radius, and is filled with dim light. The sphere moves with you, and it lasts for 1 minute or until you are incapacitated or die.
Whenever a creature (including you) ends its turn in the sphere, you can grant that creature one of these benefits:
You grant it temporary hit points equal to 1d6 plus your cleric level.
You end one effect on it causing it to be charmed or frightened.

entries.name: Twilight Domain 6th-Level Feature
entries: As a bonus action when you are in dim light or darkness, you can magically grant yourself a flying speed equal to your walking speed for 1 minute. You can use this bonus action a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Twilight Domain 8th-Level Feature
entries.description: You gain the ability to infuse your weapon strikes with divine energy. Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra {@damage ld8} radiant damage. When you reach 14th level, the extra damage increases to {@damage 2d8}.

entries.name: Twilight Sanctuary (17th Level)
entries: You and your allies have half cover while in the sphere created by your Twilight Sanctuary.

entries.name: Druid
entries: The druid class receives new features and subclasses in this section.

entries.name: Expanded Spell List
entries.description: The spells in this list expand the druid spell list in the Player's Handbook. Spells are organized by spell level, not character level. Ritual tags are indicated after spell names for spells cast as rituals. Some spells are from chapter 3 of this book (marked with an asterisk), while others appear in Xanathar's Guide to Everything.

entries.name: Spirit Summoning
entries: As a bonus action, you can expend a use of your Wild Shape feature to cast the {@spell Find Familiar|PHB} spell, without material components. The spirit assumes an animal form and remains with you until the spell ends.
This ability replaces your usual 2nd-level druid feature.

entries.name: 4th-level druid feature
entries: Whenever you reach a level in this class that grants the Ability Score Improvement feature, you can replace one cantrip you learned from this class's Spellcasting feature with another cantrip from the druid spell list.

entries.name: Druid Circle
entries.description: At 2nd level, a druid gains the Druid Circle feature, which offers you the choice of a subclass.

entries.name: Circle of Spores
entries: Druids of the Circle of Spores find beauty in decay. They see within mold and other fungi the ability to transform lifeless material into abundant, albeit somewhat strange, life.

These druids believe that life and death are parts of a grand cycle, with one leading to the other and then back again. Death isn't the end of life, but instead a change of state that sees life shift into a new form.
Druids of this circle have a complex relationship with the undead. They see nothing inherently wrong with undeath, which they consider to be a companion to life and death.

But these druids believe that the natural cycle is healthiest when each segment of it is vibrant and changing. Undead that seek to replace all life with undeath, or that try to avoid passing to a final rest, violate the cycle and must be thwarted.

entries.name: Circle of Spores Feature
entries: At 2nd level, you learn the {@item Chill Touch|PHB} cantrip.
You gain access to the spells listed for that level in the {@table Circle of Spores Spells table|tasha's-cauldron-of-everything.class.circle-of-spores-spells-table}.
Once you gain access to one of these spells, you always have it prepared, and it doesn't count against the number of spells you can prepare each day.
If you gain access to a spell that doesn't appear on the druid spell list, the spell is nevertheless a druid spell for you.

entries.name: Halo of Spores
entries.description: You are surrounded by invisible, necrotic spores that are harmless until you unleash them on a creature nearby. When a creature you can see moves into a space within 10 feet of you or starts its turn there, you can use your reaction to deal {@damage 1d4} necrotic damage to that creature unless it succeeds on a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC.

saving_throw.description: The creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC to avoid damage.

entries.name: Symbiotic Entity
entries: You gain the ability to channel magic into your spores. As an action, you can expend a use of your Wild Shape feature to awaken those spores, rather than transforming into a beast form, and you gain 4 temporary hit points for each level you have in this class.
While this feature is active, you gain the following benefits:

items: When you deal your Halo of Spores damage, roll the damage die a second time and add it to the total.
Your melee weapon attacks deal an extra {@damage 1d6} necrotic damage to any target they hit.

entries: These benefits last for 10 minutes, until you lose all these temporary hit points, or until you use your Wild Shape again.

entries.name: Trigger
entries.value: Expend a use of Wild Shape feature

entries.name: Conditions
entries.value: Lose all temporary hit points or use Wild Shape again

entries.name: Fungal Infestation
entries: Your spores gain the ability to infest a corpse and animate it. If a beast or a humanoid that is Small or Medium dies within 10 feet of you, you can use your reaction to animate it, causing it to stand up immediately with 1 hit point.

items: The creature uses the {@item Zombie|monster-manual} stat block.
It remains animate for 1 hour, after which time it collapses and dies.

entries.name: Combat Behavior
entries: The zombie's turn comes immediately after yours. It obeys your mental commands, and the only action it can take is the Attack action, making one melee attack.

entries.name: Usage
entries: You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses of it when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Halo of Spores
entries: As a bonus action while your Symbiotic Entity feature is active, you can hurl spores up to 30 feet away, where they swirl in a 10-foot cube for 1 minute. The spores disappear if you use this feature again, dismiss them as a bonus action, or if your Symbiotic Entity feature is no longer active.

Whenever a creature moves into the cube or starts its turn there, that creature takes {@damage 1d6} necrotic damage, unless the creature succeeds on a {@saving_throw Constitution} save against your spell save DC. A creature can take this damage no more than once per turn.
While the cube of spores persists, you can't use your Halo of Spores reaction.

entries.name: 14th-level Circle of Spores Feature
entries.description: You can't be blinded, deafened, frightened, or poisoned, and any critical hit against you counts as a normal hit instead, unless you're incapacitated.

entries.name: Star Chart
entries: You create a Tiny object that serves as a spellcasting focus for your druid spells. Determine its form by rolling on the Star Map table or choosing one.

entries.name: Benefits While Holding the Map
entries: You know the {@spell Guidance} cantrip.
You have the {@spell Guiding Bolt} spell prepared. It counts as a druid spell for you and doesn't count against your spell preparation limit.
You can cast {@spell Guiding Bolt} without expending a spell slot. You can do so a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
If you lose the map, you can perform a 1-hour ceremony to create a replacement. This ceremony can be performed during a short or long rest and destroys the previous map.

entries.name: Constellations
entries: As a bonus action, you can expend a use of your Wild Shape feature to take on a starry form. While in this form, you retain your game statistics, but your body becomes luminous, shedding bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet. This form lasts 10 minutes and ends if you dismiss it, are incapacitated, die, or use this feature again.

items.name: Archer
entries: A constellation of an archer appears on you. When you activate this form, and as a bonus action on your subsequent turns while it lasts, you can make a ranged spell attack hurling a luminous arrow targeting one creature within 60 feet. On a hit, the attack deals {@damage 1d8 + Wisdom modifier} radiant damage.

items.name: Chalice
entries: A constellation of a life-giving goblet appears on you. Whenever you cast a spell using a spell slot that restores hit points to a creature, you or another creature within 30 feet of you can regain hit points equal to {@damage 1d8 + Wisdom modifier}.

items.name: Dragon
entries: A constellation of a wise dragon appears on you. When you make an Intelligence or Wisdom check or a Constitution saving throw to maintain concentration on a spell, you can treat a roll of 9 or lower on the d20 as a 10.

entries.content: 2 A stone tablet with fine holes drilled through it
3 A speckled owlbear hide, tooled with raised marks
4 A collection of maps bound in an ebony cover
5 A crystal that projects starry patterns when placed before a light
6 Glass disks that depict constellations

entries.name: Star Map
entries: Whenever you finish a long rest, you can consult your Star Map for omens. When you do so, roll a die. Until you finish your next long rest, you gain access to a special reaction based on whether you rolled an even or an odd number on the die:

entries.name: Weal (even)
entries: Whenever a creature you can see within 30 feet of you is about to make an attack roll, a saving throw, or an ability check, you can use your reaction to roll a d6 and add the number rolled to the total.

entries.name: Woe (odd)
entries: Whenever a creature you can see within 30 feet of you is about to make an attack roll, a saving throw, or an ability check, you can use your reaction to roll a d6 and subtract the number rolled from the total.
You can use this reaction a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Starry Form Improvements
entries: The ld8 of the Archer and the Chalice becomes 2d8
While the Dragon is active, you have a flying speed of 20 feet and can hover

entries.name: Constellation Changes
entries: At the start of each of your turns while in your Starry Form, you can change which constellation glimmers on your body

entries.name: 14th-Level Circle of the Stars Feature
entries.description: While in your Starry Form, you become partially incorporeal, giving you resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage.

entries.name: Circle of Wildfire
entries: Druids within the Circle of Wildfire bond with a primal spirit that embodies both destructive and creative power. This connection allows them to create controlled flames that can destroy one thing while fostering life in another.
The philosophy of this circle emphasizes that destruction can be a catalyst for creation, such as how a forest fire clears debris to promote new growth.

entries.name: Circle of Wildfire Feature (2nd Level)
entries: You have formed a bond with a wildfire spirit, a primal being of creation and destruction. Your link with this spirit grants you access to some spells when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown on the Circle of Wildfire Spells table.
Once you gain access to one of these spells, you always have it prepared, and it doesn't count against the number of spells you can prepare each day.
If you gain access to a spell that doesn't appear on the druid spell list, the spell is nonetheless a druid spell for you.

entries.name: Wildfire Spirit
entries: Small elemental
Armor Class 13 (natural armor)
Hit Points 5 + five times your druid level
Speed 30 ft., fly 30 ft. (hover)

entries.name: Damage Immunities
entries: fire

entries.name: Senses
entries: darkvision 60 ft., passive Perception 12

entries.name: Languages
entries: understands the languages you speak

entries.name: Bonus to Damage or Healing Rolls
entries.content: Whenever you cast a spell that deals fire damage or restores hit points while your wildfire spirit is summoned, roll a d8, and you gain a bonus equal to the number rolled to one damage or healing roll of the spell.

entries.name: Spell Origin
entries.content: When you cast a spell with a range other than self, the spell can originate from you or your wildfire spirit.

entries.name: Death to Flame
entries: You gain the ability to turn death into magical flames that can heal or incinerate. When a Small or larger creature dies within 30 feet of you or your wildfire spirit, a harmless spectral flame springs forth in the dead creature's space and flickers there for 1 minute.

When a creature you can see enters that space, you can use your reaction to extinguish the spectral flame there and either heal the creature or deal fire damage to it. The healing or damage equals {@damage 2d10} + your Wisdom modifier.
You can use this reaction a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: 14th-level Circle of Wildfire Feature
entries: When reduced to 0 hit points and unconscious, if your wildfire spirit is within 120 feet, you can cause it to drop to 0 hit points. You regain half your maximum hit points and immediately rise to your feet.
Once used, this feature cannot be used again until after a long rest.

entries.name: New Features and Subclasses
entries: The fighter class receives new features and sub­ classes in this section.

entries.name: Fighting Styles
entries.description: When you choose a fighting style, the following styles are added to your list of options.

entries.name: Blind Fighting
entries.description: You have blindsight with a range of 10 feet. Within that range, you can effectively see anything that isn't behind total cover, even if you're blinded or in darkness. Moreover, you can see an invisible creature within that range, unless the creature successfully hides from you.

entries.name: Maneuver Selection
entries: You learn one maneuver of your choice from among those available to the Battle Master archetype.

If a maneuver you use requires your target to make a saving throw to resist the maneuver's effects, the saving throw DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice).

entries.name: Superiority Die
entries: You gain one superiority die, which is a d6 (this die is added to any superiority dice you have from another source).
A superiority die is expended when you use it. You regain your expended superiority dice when you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Drawing Thrown Weapons
entries: You can draw a weapon that has the thrown property as part of the attack you make with the weapon.

entries.name: Thrown Weapon Damage Bonus
entries: When you hit with a ranged attack using a thrown weapon, you gain a +2 bonus to the damage roll.

entries.name: Unarmed Fighting
entries.description: Your unarmed strikes can deal bludgeoning damage equal to {@damage 1d6} + your Strength modifier on a hit. If you aren't wielding any weapons or a shield when you make the attack roll, the d6 becomes a d8.

entries.name: Grapple Damage
entries.description: At the start of each of your turns, you can deal {@damage 1d4} bludgeoning damage to one creature grappled by you.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: Whenever you reach a level in this class that grants the Ability Score Improvement feature, you can do one of the following, as you shift the focus of your martial practice: Replace a fighting style you know with another fighting style available to fighters. If you know any maneuvers from the Battle Master archetype, you can replace one maneuver you know with a different maneuver.

items: Replace a fighting style you know with another fighting style available to fighters.
If you know any maneuvers from the Battle Master archetype, you can replace one maneuver you know with a different maneuver.

entries.name: Maneuver Options
entries.content: If you have access to maneuvers, the following maneuvers are added to the list of options available to you. Maneuvers are available to Battle Masters but also to characters who have a special feature like the {@feature Superior Technique} or the {@feat Martial Adept}.

items: Characters with the {@feature Superior Technique} feature
Characters with the {@feat Martial Adept} feat

entries.name: Ambush
entries.description: When you make a Dexterity (Stealth) check or an initiative roll, you can expend one superiority die and add the die to the roll, provided you aren't incapacitated.

entries.name: Bait and Switch
entries: When you're within 5 feet of a creature on your turn, you can expend one superiority die and switch places with that creature, provided you spend at least 5 feet of movement and the creature is willing and isn't incapacitated. This movement doesn't provoke opportunity attacks.

items: Until the start of your next turn, you or the other creature (your choice) gains a bonus to AC equal to the number rolled.

entries.name: BRACE
entries.content: When a creature you can see moves into the reach you have with the melee weapon you're wielding, you can use your reaction to expend one superiority die and make one attack against the creature, using that weapon. If the attack hits, add the superiority die to the weapon's damage roll.

entries: When you make a Charisma (Intimidation), Charisma (Performance), or Charisma (Persuasion) check, you can expend one superiority die and add the superiority die to the ability check.

entries.name: Grappling Strike
entries.description: Immediately after you hit a creature with a melee attack on your turn, you can expend one superiority die and then try to grapple the target as a bonus action (see the Player's Handbook for rules on grappling). Add the superiority die to your {@check Strength (Athletics)}.

entries.name: TACTICAL ASSESSMENT
entries: When you make an Intelligence (Investigation), an Intelligence (History), or a Wisdom (Insight) check, you can expend one superiority die and add the superiority die to the ability check.

entries.name: Martial Archetype Feature
entries: At 3rd level, a fighter gains the Martial Archetype feature, which offers you the choice of a subclass.
Available subclasses: Psi Warrior and Rune Knight.

entries.name: Lore
entries: A Psi Warrior is a fighter who augments their physical might with psi-infused weapon strikes, telekinetic lashes, and barriers of mental force. Many githyanki train to become such warriors, as do some of the most disciplined high elves. In the world of Eberron, many young kalashtar dream of becoming Psi Warriors.

As a Psi Warrior, you might have honed your psionic abilities through solo discipline, unlocked it under the tutelage of a master, or refined it at an academy dedicated to wielding the mind's power as both weapon and shield.

entries.name: Psionic Energy
entries.description: You gain Psionic Energy dice (d6) equal to twice your proficiency bonus. These dice fuel psionic powers. Expended dice are regained on a long rest, or as a bonus action (once per short/long rest). At 5th level, dice become d8; 11th level, d10; 17th level, d12.

entries.name: Power Usage
entries.description: Powers may expend Psionic Energy dice. A power cannot be used if its required die is expended. All dice are replenished on a long rest.

items.name: Protective Field
items.description: When you or a visible creature within 30 feet takes damage, you can use your reaction to expend a Psionic Energy die. Roll it, then reduce the damage by the roll + Intelligence modifier (minimum 1).

items.name: Psionic Strike
items.description: On your turn, after hitting a target with a weapon attack and dealing damage, expend a Psionic Energy die. Roll it and deal force damage equal to the roll + Intelligence modifier.

items.name: Telekinetic Movement
items.description: As an action, move a visible target (Large or smaller object/creature) up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space. Tiny objects can be moved to/from your hand. Requires a long/short rest before reuse, or expend a Psionic Energy die to use again.

entries.name: Psi-Powered Leap
entries.description: As a bonus action, you can propel your body with your mind. You gain a flying speed equal to twice your walking speed until the end of the current turn. Once you take this bonus action, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest, unless you expend a Psionic Energy die to take it again.
entries.usage: Bonus action (once per rest, with Psionic Energy die recharge)

entries.name: Telekinetic Thrust
entries.description: When you deal damage to a target with your Psionic Strike, you can force the target to make a Strength saving throw against a DC equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Intelligence modifier. If the save fails, you can knock the target prone or move it up to 10 feet in any direction horizontally.

entries.name: GUARDED MIND
entries: At 10th level, the psionic energy flowing through you has bolstered your mind. You have resistance to psychic damage.
Moreover, if you start your turn charmed or frightened, you can expend a Psionic Energy die and end every effect on yourself subjecting you to those conditions.

entries.name: Telekinetic Shield
entries: As a bonus action, you can choose creatures (which can include you) that you can see within 30 feet of you, up to a number of creatures equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of one creature). Each chosen creature is protected by half cover for 1 minute or until you're incapacitated.
Once you take this bonus action, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest, unless you expend a Psionic Energy die to take it again.

entries.name: Rune Knight
entries.lore: Rune Knights enhance their martial prowess using the supernatural power of runes, an ancient practice originating with giants. Rune cutters are found among giant families, and practitioners typically learn their methods from such artisans through direct study, encountering runes carved in natural formations, learning from sages, or meeting giants in person. They apply magic runes to empower their equipment.

entries.description: You gain proficiency with smith's tools.
You learn to speak, read, and write Giant.

entries.name: Cloud Rune
entries: While wearing or carrying an object inscribed with this rune, you have advantage on Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) checks and Charisma (Deception) checks.

When you or a creature you can see within 30 feet of you is hit by an attack roll, you can use your reaction to invoke the rune and choose a different creature within 30 feet of you, other than the attacker. The chosen creature becomes the target of the attack, using the same roll. This magic can transfer the attack's effects regardless of the attack's range. Once you invoke this rune, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Fire Rune
entries: While wearing or carrying an object inscribed with this rune, your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses your proficiency with a tool.

When you hit a creature with an attack using a weapon, you can invoke the rune to summon fiery shackles: the target takes an extra 2d6 fire damage, and it must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be restrained for 1 minute. While restrained by the shackles, the target takes 2d6 fire damage at the start of each of its turns. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, banishing the shackles on a success. Once you invoke this rune, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Frost Rune
entries: While wearing or carrying an object inscribed with this rune, you have advantage on Wisdom (Animal Handling) checks and Charisma (Intimidation) checks.

You can invoke the rune as a bonus action to increase your sturdiness. For 10 minutes, you gain a +2 bonus to all ability checks and saving throws that use Strength or Constitution. Once you invoke this rune, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Stone Rune
entries: While wearing or carrying an object inscribed with this rune, you have advantage on Wisdom (Insight) checks, and you have darkvision out to a range of 120 feet.

When a creature you can see ends its turn within 30 feet of you, you can use your reaction to invoke the rune and force the creature to make a Wisdom saving throw. Unless the save succeeds, the creature is charmed by you for 1 minute. While charmed in this way, the creature has a speed of 0 and is incapacitated, descending into a dreamy stupor. The creature repeats the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. Once you invoke this rune, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Hill Rune
entries: While wearing or carrying an object that bears this rune, you have advantage on saving throws against being poisoned, and you have resistance against poison damage.

You can invoke the rune as a bonus action, gaining resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage for 1 minute. Once you invoke this rune, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Storm Rune
entries: While wearing or carrying an object inscribed with this rune, you have advantage on Intelligence (Arcana) checks, and you can't be surprised as long as you aren't incapacitated.

You can invoke the rune as a bonus action to enter a prophetic state for 1 minute or until you're incapacitated. Until the state ends, when you or another creature you can see within 60 feet of you makes an attack roll, a saving throw, or an ability check, you can use your reaction to cause the roll to have advantage or disadvantage. Once you invoke this rune, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: GIANT'S MIGHT
entries.description:
You have learned how to imbue yourself with the might of giants. As a bonus action, you magically gain the following benefits, which last for 1 minute:

- If you are smaller than Large, you become Large, along with anything you are wearing. If you lack the room to become Large, your size doesn't change.
- You have advantage on Strength checks and Strength saving throws.
- Once on each of your turns, one of your attacks with a weapon or an unarmed strike can deal an extra 1d6 damage to a target on a hit.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses of it when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Runic Shield
entries: When another creature you can see within 60 feet of you is hit by an attack roll, you can use your reaction to force the attacker to reroll the d20 and use the new roll.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: GREAT STATURE
entries: At 10th level, the magic of your runes permanently alters you. When you gain this feature, roll 3d4. You grow a number of inches in height equal to the roll.
Moreover, the extra damage you deal with your Giant's Might feature increases to 1d8 {@damage 1d8}.

entries.name: Rune Carver (15th Level)
entries.description: You can invoke each rune you know from your Rune Carver feature twice, rather than once, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: 18th-level Rune Knight feature
entries.description: You learn how to amplify your rune-powered transformation. As a result, the extra damage you deal with the Giant's Might feature increases to {@damage 1d10}. Moreover, when you use that feature, your size can increase to Huge, and while you are that size, your reach increases by 5 feet.

entries.name: Fighting Style: Archery
entries: You prefer to deal with your enemies from afar, trusting in a well-placed arrow, javelin, or sling bullet to end a fight without a response. You rely on accuracy and probably subscribe to the axiom that "those who live by the sword die by the bow."

entries.name: Fighting Style
entries: Interception

entries.name: Maneuvers
entries: Bait and Switch
Disarming Attack
Goading Attack
Grappling Strike

entries.name: Feats
entries: Alert
Observant
Sentinel
Tough

entries.text: Love, money, or some other obligation motivates you to place your own body between harm and the one you're sworn to protect. You have honed the ability to sniff out potential threats and see your charge through dangerous situations.

entries.name: BRAWLER
entries.description: When bottles start breaking and chairs start flying, you're in your element. You love a good scrap, and you've likely seen your share of them. You may or may not have formal training, and while others might call you a dirty fighter, you're still alive.

entries.name: Duelist
entries.description: You regard the duel as a proud tradition, a test of skill and wits that brings honor to those who can defeat an enemy while respecting the art. Your search for improvement is a consuming passion, and you draw on the expertise of the masters who've come before you as you work to perfect your form.

entries.name: Fighting Style
entries: Defense

entries.name: Two-Weapon Fighting Maneuvers
entries: Goading Attack
Menacing Attack
Sweeping Attack
Trip Attack

entries.name: Feats
entries: Athlete
Charger
Dual Wielder
Durable
Grappler
Savage Attacker
Tough
Weapon Master

entries.name: HOPLITE
entries.description: With spear and shield, you follow in the footsteps of the heroes of ages past. You rely on discipline and athleticism to overcome improbable odds. Whether fighting in ranks alongside your comrades or squaring off as a lone warrior, you're equal to the task.

features.name: Fighting Style
entries: Defense
Thrown Weapon Fighting

features.name: Maneuvers
entries: Brace
Lunging Attack
Parry
Precision Attack

features.name: Feats
entries: Athlete
Grappler
Polearm Master
Sentinel
Shield Master

entries.text: When the cavalry is called in, that means you. You ride out to greet your enemy with the point of your weapon. As you charge, the ground trembles, and only the heaviest blows can deter you.

entries.name: Description
entries: Where others rely on steel, you've got your fists. Whether through training or experience, you've developed a superior technique that can help you overcome an enemy in an up-close fight.

entries.name: Fighting Style
entries: Great Weapon Fighting: {@maneuver Menacing Attack}, {@maneuver Pushing Attack}, {@maneuver Sweeping Attack}

entries.name: Description
entries: Subtlety is not your style. You're trained to get straight into the fighting, busting through enemy lines and applying tremendous pressure quickly. Those who ignore you in combat do so at their peril.

entries.name: Fighting Style
entries: Archery
Thrown Weapon Fighting*

entries: Ambush,*
Bait and Switch,*
Distracting Strike
Quick Toss*

entries: Alert
Dual Wielder
Mobile
Skulker

entries.content: You thrive amid the chaos of battle. You use your mobility and versatility in combat to soften your adversaries and disrupt their formations. An enemy's plan rarely survives contact with you.

entries.name: Class Features
entries: Maneuvers: Commander's Strike, Commanding Presence, Maneuvering Attack, Rally, Tactical Assessment
Feats: Inspiring Leader, Keen Mind, Linguist

entries.text: To you, battles unfold like a game of chess. You understand that strength and speed are important in a fight, but it takes intellect and experience to know how best to apply them. That's where you come in.

items: The monk class receives new features and subclasses.

entries.name: Monk 2nd-Level Feature
entries: You train yourself to use a variety of weapons as monk weapons, not just simple melee weapons and shortswords. Whenever you finish a short or long rest, you can touch one weapon, focus your ki on it, and then count that weapon as a monk weapon until you use this feature again.

items: The weapon must be a simple or martial weapon.
You must be proficient with it.
It must lack the heavy and special properties.

entries.name: 3rd-level monk feature
entries.description: If you spend 1 ki point or more as part of your action on your turn, you can make one attack with an unarmed strike or a monk weapon as a bonus action before the end of the turn.
requirements: Spending 1 ki point or more as part of your action on your turn
benefits: Make one attack with an unarmed strike or a monk weapon as a bonus action before the end of your turn
entries.usage: Spending ki points during your action triggers this bonus action attack.

entries.name: Martial Arts Die
entries: As an action, you can spend 2 ki points and roll a Martial Arts die. You regain a number of hit points equal to the number rolled plus your proficiency bonus.

entries.name: Monastic Tradition
entries: At 3rd level, a monk gains the Monastic Tradition feature, which offers you the choice of a subclass.
The following options are available to you when making that choice: Way of Mercy or Way of the Astral Self.

entries.name: Monk (Way of Mercy)
entries.description: Monks of the Way of Mercy manipulate the life force of others to aid the needy, serving as wandering physicians to the poor and hurt. They swiftly end lives beyond their capacity to help, acting as grim arbiters of mercy. They often wear robes with deep cowls and conceal their faces with masks, presenting as faceless agents of life and death.

entries.name: 3rd-Level Feature
entries: You gain proficiency in the Insight and Medicine skills, and you gain proficiency with the herbalism kit.

items: A special mask, which you often wear when using the features of this subclass.
You determine its appearance, or generate it randomly by rolling on the Merciful Mask table.

entries.name: Implements of Mercy
entries: The mask is a unique item associated with this subclass, with its appearance determined by the player or through the Merciful Mask table.
Raven
Blank and white
Crying visage
Laughing visage
Skull
Butterfly

entries.name: Hand of Healing
entries: As an action, you can spend {@ki 1} ki point to touch a creature and restore a number of hit points equal to a roll of your Martial Arts die + your Wisdom modifier.
When you use your Flurry of Blows, you can replace one of the unarmed strikes with a use of this feature without spending a ki point for the healing.

entries.name: Way of Mercy 3rd-Level Feature
entries.description: You use your ki to inflict wounds. When you hit a creature with an unarmed strike, you can spend 1 ki point to deal extra necrotic {@damage 1d6} damage equal to one roll of your Martial Arts die + your Wisdom modifier. You can use this feature only once per turn.

entries.name: Flurry of Comfort and Hurt
entries: When using Flurry of Blows, you can replace each unarmed strike with a use of your Hand of Healing, without spending ki points for the healing.
When making an unarmed strike with Flurry of Blows, you can use Hand of Harm with that strike without spending the ki point for Hand of Harm. You can still use Hand of Harm only once per turn.

entries.name: Reviving the Deceased
entries: As an action, you can touch the corpse of a creature that died within the past 24 hours and expend 5 ki points. The creature then returns to life, regaining a number of hit points equal to {@damage 4d10} + your Wisdom modifier.

If the creature died while subject to any of the following conditions, it revives with them removed: {@condition blinded}, {@condition deafened}, {@condition paralyzed}, {@condition poisoned}, and {@condition stunned}.

entries.name: Cooldown
items: Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Way of the Astral Self
entries.description: A monk who follows this path believes their physical body is an illusion. They perceive their ki as a representation of their true astral self, which can manifest as a force of order or chaos. Training varies between monasteries: some focus on protecting the weak through disciplined control, while others train aspirants to harness their astral self for service to powerful entities.

entities.name: 3rd-level Way of the Astral Self  feature

entries.name: Astral Arms
entries: As a bonus action, you can spend 1 ki point to summon the arms of your astral self. Each creature of your choice that you can see within 10 feet of you must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 2d8} force damage.
The spectral arms hover near your shoulders or surround your arms (your choice) for 10 minutes. They vanish if you are incapacitated or die.
While the spectral arms are present, you gain the following benefits:

items: You can use your Wisdom modifier in place of your Strength modifier for Strength checks and Strength saving throws.
You can use the spectral arms to make unarmed strikes.
When you make an unarmed strike with the arms on your turn, your reach for it is 5 feet greater than normal.
Unarmed strikes made with the arms can use your Wisdom modifier in place of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for attack and damage rolls. Their damage type is force.

entries.name: Visage of the Astral Self
entries: You can summon the visage of your astral self. {@action bonus action} or as part of the bonus action you take to activate Arms of the Astral Self, you can spend 1 ki point to summon this visage for {@duration 10 minutes}. It vanishes early if you are {@condition incapacitated} or {@condition die}.

entries.name: Astral Sight
entries: You can see normally in darkness, both magical and nonmagical, to a distance of {@distance 120 feet}.

entries.name: Wisdom of the Spirit
entries: You have {@advantage} on Wisdom (Insight) and Charisma (Intimidation) checks.

entries.name: Word of the Spirit
entries: When you speak, you can direct your words to a creature of your choice that you can see within {@distance 60 feet} of you, making it so only that creature can hear you. Alternatively, you can amplify your voice so that all creatures within {@distance 600 feet} can hear you.

entries.name: Deflect Energy
entries.description: When you take acid, cold, fire, force, lightning, or thunder damage, you can use your reaction to deflect it. The damage you take is reduced by 1d10 + your Wisdom modifier (minimum reduction of 1).

entries.name: Empowered Arms
entries.description: Once on each of your turns when you hit a target with the Arms of the Astral Self, you can deal extra damage to the target equal to your Martial Arts die.

entries.name: Awakened Astral Self
entries: At 17th level, your connection to your astral self is complete, allowing you to unleash its full potential. As a bonus action, you can spend 5 ki points to summon the arms, visage, and body of your astral self and awaken it for 10 minutes. This awakening ends early if you are incapacitated or die.

items: While your astral self is awakened, you gain the following benefits.
The duration of the awakening is 10 minutes.
The awakening ends early if you are incapacitated or die.

entries.name: Multiclassing
entries: You can't multiclass with this class.

entries.name: Astral Self Description
entries: The astral self is a translucent embodiment of the monk's soul. It can reflect aspects of the monk's background, ideals, flaws, and bonds, and does not necessarily resemble the monk physically. Examples include a monk's astral self resembling a minotaur (symbolizing strength) or having gossamer arms and a delicate visage (symbolizing gentle beauty). Each astral self is unique, and some monks are known more for their astr. self's appearance than their physical form.
When choosing this path, consider quirks defining your monk. Motivations such as obsession, justice, or selfish desires may manifest in the form of your astral self.

items.name: Armor of the Spirit
entries: Gain a +2 bonus to Armor Class.

items.name: Astral Barrage
entries: Whenever you use the Extra Attack feature to attack twice, you can instead attack three times if all attacks are made with your astral arms.

entries.name: Paladin Class Features
entries.content: The paladin class receives new features and subclasses in this section.

entries.name: Cantrip Selection
entries.text: You learn two cantrips of your choice from the cleric spell list. They count as paladin spells for you, and Charisma is your spellcasting ability for them.

entries.name: Cantrip Replacement
entries.text: Whenever you gain a level in this class, you can replace one of these cantrips with another cantrip from the cleric spell list.

entries.name: Interception
entries.description: When a creature you can see hits a target (other than you) within 5 feet of you with an attack, you can use your reaction to reduce the damage the target takes. {@damage 1d8 + proficiency} (to a minimum of 0 damage).

items: You must be wielding a shield
You must be wielding a simple or martial weapon

entries.effect: The target takes reduced damage based on your roll and proficiency bonus, with a minimum of 0 damage.

entries.name: Channel Divinity (3rd Level)
entries: As a bonus action, you touch your holy symbol, utter a prayer, and regain one expended spell slot. The level of the spell slot cannot exceed half your proficiency bonus (rounded up).
You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Fighting Style Replacement
entries.description: Whenever you reach a level in this class that grants the Ability Score Improvement feature, you can replace a fighting style you know with another fighting style available to paladins. This replacement represents a shift of focus in your martial practice.

entries.name: Sacred Oath
entries: At 3rd level, a paladin gains the Sacred Oath feature, which offers you the choice of a subclass.

entries.name: Oath of Glory
entries: Paladins who take the Oath of Glory believe they and their companions are destined to achieve glory through deeds of heroism. They train diligently and encourage their companions so they're all ready when destiny calls.

items: Actions over Words. Strive to be known by glorious deeds, not words.
Challenges Are but Tests. Face hardships with courage, and encourage your allies to face them with you.
Hone the Body. Like raw stone, your body must be worked so its potential can be realized.
Discipline the Soul. You must marshal the discipline to overcome failings within yourself that threaten to dim the glory of you and your friends.

entries.name: Oath of Glory (3rd Level)
entries.text: You gain oath spells at the paladin levels listed in the {@table Oath of Glory Spells} table. See the {@feature tasha's-cauldron-of-everything.class.sacred-oath} class feature for how oath spells work.

entries: You emanate an aura that fills you and your companions with supernatural speed, allowing you to race across a battlefield in formation. Your walking speed increases by 10 feet. In addition, if you aren't incapacitated, the walking speed of any ally who starts their turn within 5 feet of you increases by 10 feet until the end of that turn.
When you reach 18th level in this class, the range of the aura increases to 10 feet.

entries.name: Defense to Strike Reaction
entries.description: When you or another creature you can see within 10 feet of you is hit by an attack roll, you can use your reaction to grant a bonus to the target's AC against that attack, potentially causing it to miss. The bonus equals your Charisma modifier (minimum of +1). If the attack misses, you can make one weapon attack against the attacker as part of this reaction, provided the attacker is within your weapon's range. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Otherworldly Presence
entries.description: You gain advantage on all Charisma checks.

entries.name: Attack Reroll
entries.description: Once on each of your turns when you make a weapon attack and miss, you can cause that attack to hit instead.

entries.name: Saving Throw Reroll
entries.description: If you fail a saving throw, you can use your reaction to reroll it. You must use this new roll.

entries.name: Bonus Action Limitation
entries.description: Once you use this bonus action, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest, unless you expend a 5th-level spell slot to use it again.

entries.name: Oath of the Watchers
entries: The Oath of the Watchers binds paladins to protect mortal realms from the predations of extraplanar creatures, many of which can lay waste to mortal soldiers. Thus, the Watchers hone their minds, spirits, and bodies to be the ultimate weapons against such threats.

Paladins who follow the Watchers' oath are ever vigilant in spotting the influence of extraplanar forces, often establishing a network of spies and informants to gather information on suspected cults. To a Watcher, keeping a healthy suspicion and awareness about one's surroundings is as natural as wearing armor in battle.

entries.name: Oath of the Watchers
entries: A paladin who assumes the Oath of the Watchers swears to safeguard mortal realms from otherworldly threats.
Vigilance. The threats you face are cunning, powerful, and subversive. Be ever alert for their corruption.
Loyalty. Never accept gifts or favors from fiends or those who truck with them. Stay true to your order, your comrades, and your duty.
Discipline. You are the shield against the endless terrors that lie beyond the stars. Your blade must be forever sharp and your mind keen to survive what lies beyond.

entries.name: Oath of the Watchers 3rd-Level Feature
entries: You gain oath spells at the paladin levels listed in the Oath of the Watchers table.
See the {@feature tasha's-cauldron-of-everything.class.sacred-oath} class feature for how oath spells work.

entries.name: Watcher's Will
entries.content: As an action, you can choose a number of creatures you can see within 30 feet of you, up to a number equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one creature). For 1 minute, you and the chosen creatures have advantage on Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws.

entries.name: Abjure the Extraplanar
entries.content: As an action, you present your holy symbol and each aberration, celestial, elemental, fey, or fiend within 30 feet of you that can hear you must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is turned for 1 minute or until it takes damage. A turned creature must spend its turns trying to move as far away from you as it can, and it can't willingly end its move in a space within 30 feet of you. For its action, it can use only the Dash action or try to escape from an effect that prevents it from moving. If there's nowhere to move, the creature can take the Dodge action.

entries.name: Aura of Alertness
entries.description: You emit an aura of alertness while you aren't incapacitated. When you and any creatures of your choice within 10 feet of you roll initiative, you all gain a bonus to initiative equal to your proficiency bonus.

entries.name: At 18th Level
entries: The range of this aura increases to 30 feet.

entries.name: Vigilant Rebuttal
entries: When you or a creature you can see within 30 feet of you succeeds on an Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma saving throw, you can use your reaction to deal {@damage 2d8+CHA} force damage to the creature that forced the saving throw.
This feature replaces the Oath of the Watchers' 14th-level feature.

entries.name: Requirements
items: You or a visible creature within 30 feet succeeds on an Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma saving throw
The creature that forced the saving throw is within line of effect

entries.name: Action Details
items: Target: The creature that forced the saving throw

entries.name: Divine Spark of the Watchers
entries.description: As a bonus action, you gain the following benefits for 1 minute:

entries.name: Truesight
entries: You gain truesight with a range of 120 feet.

entries.name: Advantage on Attacks
entries: You have advantage on attack rolls against aberrations, celestials, elementals, fey, and fiends.

entries.name: Banishment on Hit
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack and deal damage, you can force it to make a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the creature is magically banished to its native plane of existence if it's currently not there. On a successful save, the creature can't be banished by this feature for 24 hours.

entries.name: Usage Limitation
entries: Once you use this bonus action, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest, unless you expend a 5th-level spell slot to use it again.

entries.name: Deft Explorer
