entries.description: 1st-level ranger feature, which replaces the Natural Explorer feature.
entries: You are an unsurpassed explorer and survivor, both in the wilderness and in dealing with others on your travels.
You gain the Canny benefit below, and you gain an additional benefit below when you reach 6th level and 10th level in this class.

entries.name: Skill Proficiency Bonus
entries.text: Choose one of your skill proficiencies. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses the chosen skill.

entries.name: Additional Languages
entries.text: You can also speak, read, and write two additional languages of your choice.

entries: Your walking speed increases by 5.
You gain a climbing speed equal to your walking speed.
You gain a swimming speed equal to your walking speed.

entries.name: Temporary Hit Points
entries.description: As an action, you can give yourself a number of temporary hit points equal to 1d8 + your Wisdom modifier (minimum of 1 temporary hit point). You can use this action a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Exhaustion Reduction
entries.description: In addition, whenever you finish a short rest, your exhaustion level, if any, is decreased by 1.

entries.name: Favored Foe
entries: A 1st-level ranger feature that replaces the Favored Enemy feature and works with the Foe Slayer feature.
When you hit a creature with an attack roll, you can mark the target as your favored enemy for 1 minute or until you lose concentration (as if you were concentrating on a spell).
The first time on each of your turns that you hit the favored enemy and deal damage to it (including when you mark it), you can increase that damage by {@damage 1d4}.
You can use this feature to mark a favored enemy a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
This feature's extra damage increases when you reach certain levels in this class: to {@damage 1d6} at 6th level and to {@damage 1d8} at 14th level.

entries.name: Multiclassing
entries: This feature is exclusive to the ranger class and does not grant cross-class benefits.

entries.name: DRUIDIC WARRIOR
entries.description: A hybrid class combining druidic magic with martial prowess, allowing spellcasting using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability.

abilities.name: Cantrips
entries: You learn two cantrips of your choice from the druid spell list. They count as ranger spells for you, and Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for them.

abilities.name: Replacing Cantrips
entries: When you gain a level in this class, you can replace one of these cantrips with another cantrip from the druid spell list.

entries.name: Primal Awareness Spells
entries: At 3rd level, you learn additional spells from the Primal Awareness Spells table. These spells do not count toward the number of ranger spells you know.

items: The spells are acquired when you reach certain levels in this class (see the Primal Awareness Spells table).
Spells learned through this feature are not subject to the ranger's spell knowledge limit.

entries.name: Multiclassing
entries: This feature replaces the Primal Awareness feature from the Ranger's base class.

entries.name: Primal Awareness Spells
entries: You can cast each of these spells once without expending a spell slot.
Once you cast a spell in this way, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Fighting Style Replacement
entries.description: Whenever you reach a level in this class that grants the Ability Score Improvement feature, you can replace a fighting style you know with another fighting style available to rangers. This replacement represents a shift of focus in your martial practice.

data.name: Hide in Plain Sight
data.description: You draw on the powers of nature to hide yourself from view briefly. As a bonus action, you can magically become invisible, along with any equipment you are wearing or carrying, until the start of your next turn.
data.benefit: You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Fey Wanderer
entries: A Fey Wanderer is a subclass of the Ranger class, representing both the mortal and the fey realms. This subclass grants a connection to fey magic acquired through various means such as an archfey's boon, a talking tree, a magic spring, or other auspicious events.

The Fey Wanderer's fey mystique manifests as a blend of joyful mirth and formidable fury, allowing them to brighten the hearts of the downtrodden with laughter while striking terror in their foes through martial prowess.

entries.name: Feywild Psychic Damage
entries: When you hit a creature with a weapon, you can deal an extra {@damage 1d4} psychic damage to the target, which can take this extra damage only once per turn.

items: The extra damage increases to {@damage 1d6} when you reach 11th level in this class.

entries.name: Feywild Origin
entries: This magic is drawn from the gloomy hollows of the Feywild.

entries.name: Feywild Blessing Selection
entries: You possess a preternatural blessing from a fey ally or a place of fey power. Choose your blessing from the Feywild Gifts table or determine it randomly.

entries.text: Feywild Gifts table

entries.name: d6 Gift
entries.content:
While you take a short or long rest, illusory butterflies flutter around you. Choose one of the following effects:

2. Fresh, seasonal flowers sprout from your hair each dawn.
3. You faintly smell of cinnamon, lavender, nutmeg, or another comforting herb or spice.
4. Your shadow dances while no one is looking directly at it.
5. Horns or antlers sprout from your head.
6. Your skin and hair change color to match the season at each dawn.

entries.name: Supernatural Charm
entries: Whenever you make a Charisma check, you gain a bonus to the check equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of +1).

entries.name: Skill Proficiency
entries: Choose one of the following skills to gain proficiency in: Deception, Performance, or Persuasion.

entries.name: Fey Wild Mind
entries.description: You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed or frightened.

entries.name: Twist of Fate
entries.description: When you or a creature you can see within 120 feet of you succeeds on a saving throw against being charmed or frightened, you can use your reaction to force a different creature you can see within 120 feet of you to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. If the save fails, the target is charmed or frightened by you (your choice) for 1 minute. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a successful save.

entries.name: Fey Wanderer 15th-level Feature
entries: You can cast {@spell misty-step|PHB} without expending a spell slot. You can do so a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

Whenever you cast {@spell misty-step|PHB}, you can bring along one willing creature you can see within 5 feet of you. That creature teleports to an unoccupied space of your choice within 5 feet of your destination space.

entries.name: SWARMKEEPER
entries: A subclass of the Ranger class, Swarmkeepers bond with swarms of nature spirits to become potent combatants and companions. They channel the magic of their connection to the environment, forming a symbiotic relationship with their swarm.

Swarmkeepers may choose to isolate themselves as outcasts or hermits, prioritizing their bond with their swarm over social interaction. Others build communities centered around mutual benefit with those they consider part of their swarm.
The swarm serves as both a tactical asset in battle and a source of support, enhancing the ranger's capabilities through magical synergy with the swarm's collective nature spirits.

entries.name: Swarm Description
entries: A swarm of intangible nature spirits has bonded itself to you and can assist you in battle. Until you die, the swarm remains in your space, crawling on you or flying and skittering around you within your space. You determine its appearance, or you generate its appearance by rolling on the Swarm Appearance table.

entries.name: Spells
items: additional spells of 1st level or higher (see Swarmkeeper Spells table)

entries.name: Spell Details
entries: Each spell counts as a ranger spell for you, but it doesn't count against the number of ranger spells you know.

entries.name: Swarm Assistance
entries.description: Once on each of your turns, you can cause the swarm to assist you in one of the following ways, immediately after you hit a creature with an attack:

entries.name: Piercing Damage
entries.description: The attack's target takes 1d6 piercing damage from the swarm.

entries.name: Movement Effect
entries.description: The attack's target must succeed on a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC or be moved by the swarm up to 15 feet horizontally in a direction of your choice.

entries.name: Caster Movement
entries.description: You are moved by the swarm 5 feet horizontally in a direction of your choice.

entries.name: Focused Swarm Flight
entries: As a bonus action, you gain a flying speed of 10 feet and can hover. This effect lasts for 1 minute or until you are incapacitated.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Uses
items: Number of uses per long rest: {@proficiency_bonus}
Regain all uses after a long rest

entries.name: Gathered Swarm Damage Increase
entries.description: The damage dealt by Gathered Swarm increases to 1d8.

entries.name: Knock Prone on Failure
entries.description: If a creature fails its saving throw against being moved by Gathered Swarm, you can cause the swarm to knock the creature prone.

entries.name: Half Cover from Movement
entries.description: When you are moved by Gathered Swarm, you gain half cover until the start of your next turn.

entries.name: Discorporate
entries: When you take damage, you can use your reaction to give yourself resistance to that damage. You vanish into your swarm and then teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within 30 feet of you, where you reappear with the swarm.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Usage
items: Number of uses per long rest: {@proficiency_bonus}

entries.name: Beast Master Companions
entries: The Beast Master in the Player's Handbook forms a mystical bond with an animal. As an alternative, a Beast Master can take the feature below to form a bond with a special primal beast instead.

entries.name: Primal Companion
entries: A 3rd-level Beast Master feature that replaces the Ranger's Companion feature.
You magically summon a primal beast that draws strength from your bond with nature. The beast is friendly to you and your companions and obeys your commands.
Choose its stat block: Beast of the Land, Beast of the Sea, or Beast of the Sky. Each uses your proficiency bonus (PB) in several places.
Determine the kind of animal the beast is, selecting a kind appropriate for the chosen stat block.
The beast bears primal markings, indicating its mystical origin.

entries.name: Combat Behavior
entries: The beast acts during your turn. It can move and use its reaction on its own, but the only action it takes is the Dodge action, unless you take a bonus action on your turn to command it to take another action.
That action can be one from its stat block or some other action. You can also sacrifice one of your attacks when you take the Attack action to command the beast to take the Attack action.
If you are incapacitated, the beast can take any action of its choice, not just Dodge.
Choose between Beast of the Land, Beast of the Sea, or Beast of the Sky.
Each stat block uses your proficiency bonus (PB) in several places.

entries.name: Primal Markings
entries: The beast bears primal markings, indicating its mystical origin.

entries.name: BEAST OF THE LAND
entries: Medium beast
Armor Class 13 + PB (natural armor)
Hit Points 5 + five times your ranger level (the beast has a number of Hit Dice [d8s] equal to your ranger level)
Speed 40 ft., climb 40 ft.
Senses darkvision 60 ft., passive Perception 12
Languages understands the languages you speak
Challenge -
Proficiency Bonus (PB) equals your bonus

Charge. If the beast moves at least 20 feet straight toward a target and then hits it with a maul attack on the same turn, the target takes an extra 1d6 slashing damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC or be knocked prone.
Primal Bond. You can add your proficiency bonus to any ability check or saving throw that the beast makes.

entries.name: BEAST OF THE SEA
entries: Medium beast
Armor Class 13 (+1 natural armor)
Hit Points 5 + five times your ranger level (the beast has a number of Hit Dice [d8s] equal to your ranger level)
Speed 5 ft., swim 60 ft.
Senses darkvision 60 ft., passive Perception 12
Languages understands the languages you speak
Challenge -
Proficiency Bonus (PB) equals your bonus
Amphibious. The beast can breathe both air and water.
Primal Bond. You can add your proficiency bonus to any ability check or saving throw that the beast makes.

entries.name: BEAST OF THE SKY
entries: Small beast

items: Hit Points 4 + four times your ranger level (the beast has a number of Hit Dice [d6s] equal to your ranger level)

entries: Senses: darkvision 60 ft., passive Perception 12
Languages: understands the languages you speak
Challenge: not specified
Proficiency Bonus (PB) equals your bonus

entries.name: Features
entries: Flyby. The beast doesn't provoke opportunity attacks when it flies out of an enemy's reach.
Primal Bond. You can add your proficiency bonus to any ability check or saving throw that the beast makes.

entries.name: Rogue Class Features and Subclasses
entries: The rogue class receives new features and subclasses in this section.

entries.name: Optional Class Feature
entries.description: You gain class features from the Player's Handbook when reaching certain levels. This section offers an additional feature available to rogues. Unlike standard features, this one requires consultation with your DM to decide whether to gain it if you meet the level requirement specified in the feature's description.
entries.special: Consultation with your DM is required to determine if you gain this feature, contingent on meeting the level requirement noted in the feature's description.

entries.name: Bonus Action: Advantage on Next Attack Roll
entries: As a bonus action, you give yourself advantage on your next attack roll on the current turn. You can use this bonus action only if you haven't moved during this turn, and after you use the bonus, your speed is 0 until the end of the current turn.

entries.name: Roguish Archetype Feature
entries: At 3rd level, a rogue gains the Roguish Archetype feature, which offers you the choice of a subclass.

entries.name: Phantom Lore
entries: Phantoms are rogues who tap into death itself, gaining abilities tied to negative energy and ghost-like traits. They serve as effective spies and information gatherers in thieves' guilds, and may act as agents in temples of death deities or as confidants to necromancers in regions like Thay and Karrnath.

This path emphasizes connections to the afterlife, with practitioners often developing a deep understanding of death's mechanics and its role in the world. The exact methods of attaining this power vary, including sleep in graveyards, training in shadowy organizations, or study under death-deity cults.

entries.name: Echoes of Those Who Have Died
entries: When you finish a short or long rest, you can choose one skill or tool proficiency that you lack and gain it, as a ghostly presence shares its knowledge with you. You lose this proficiency when you use this feature to choose a different proficiency that you lack.

As you nudge someone closer to the grave, you can channel the power of death to harm another creature. Immediately after dealing your Sneak Attack damage to a creature on your turn, you can target a second creature that you can see within 30 feet of the first creature. Roll half the number of Sneak Attack dice for your level (round up), and the second creature takes {@damage 1d8} necrotic damage equal to the roll's total, as wails of the dead sound around them for a moment.

items: You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus.
You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Soul Trinket Creation
entries: When a creature you can see dies within 30 feet of you, you can use a reaction to create a Tiny soul trinket in your free hand. The DM determines its form or has you roll on the Trinkets table in the Player's Handbook.
You can have a maximum number of soul trinkets equal to your proficiency bonus. You cannot create a trinket if you already have the maximum number.

items.name: Enhanced Vitality
entries: While a soul trinket is on your person, you have advantage on death saving throws and Constitution saving throws.

items.name: Sneak Attack Synergy
entries: When you deal Sneak Attack damage on your turn, you can destroy one soul trinket and immediately use Wails from the Grave (PHB.Feature.WailsFromTheGrave) without expending a use of that feature.

items.name: Spirit Question
entries: As an action, you can destroy a soul trinket (regardless of its location) and ask the associated spirit one question. The spirit appears and answers in a language it knew in life. The spirit is not obligated to be truthful and knows only what it knew in life (DM determination).

entries.name: Phantom
entries.description: You can phase partially into the realm of the dead, becoming like a ghost. As a bonus action, you assume a spectral form. While in this form, you have a flying speed of 10 feet, you can hover, and attack rolls have disadvantage against you. You can also move through creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain, but you take {@damage 10 force} damage if you end your turn inside a creature or an object. You stay in this form for 10 minutes or until you end it as a bonus action. To use this feature again, you must finish a long rest or destroy one of your soul trinkets as part of the bonus action you use to activate Ghost Walk.

entries.name: Wails from the Grave (Enhanced)
entries.description: When you use your Wails from the Grave, you can deal necrotic damage to both the first and the second creature.

entries.name: Soul Trinket Appearance
entries.description: At the end of a long rest, a soul trinket {@item soul-trinket|phb.item.soul-trinket} appears in your hand if you don't have any soul trinkets.

entries.name: Class Description
entries: A Soulknife is a rogue who channels psionic power to strike and infiltrate with the mind, bypassing physical and psychic barriers. Unlike traditional rogues, they use mental abilities rather than weapons or thieves' tools. They are often employed as spies by governments and may face mistrust from other rogues due to their use of mind powers.

items: Skills: Stealth, Perception (as per rogue proficiency)

entries.name: Lore
entries: Soulknives may have latent psionic abilities that manifest under stress or through training with psychic adepts. They serve as silent guardians in ancient forests (wood elves) or githzerai in the endless war against githyanki. Their powers are tied to mental manipulation and infiltration.

entries.name: Psionic Energy
entries: You gain Psionic Energy dice equal to twice your proficiency bonus (minimum of 2). Each die is a d6 and fuels psionic powers.
Expended dice are regained after a long rest. As a bonus action, regain one expended die, but only after a short or long rest.
At 5th level, Psionic Energy dice increase to d8; 11th level to d10; 17th level to d12.

entries.name: Psi-Bolstered Knack
entries: On a failed ability check using a proficiency, roll a Psionic Energy die. Add the roll to the check, potentially turning failure into success. Expend the die only if the roll succeeds.

entries.name: Psychic Whispers
entries: As an action, choose up to your proficiency bonus creatures within line of sight. Roll a Psionic Energy die; the duration is equal to the number rolled (in hours).
Telepathic communication requires line of sight within 1 mile. Creatures without language proficiency cannot use this ability.
First use after a long rest does not expend the die. Subsequent uses expend the die.

entries.name: Homing Strikes
entries.description: If you make an attack roll with your Psychic Blades and miss the target, you can roll one Psionic Energy die and add the number rolled to the attack roll. If this causes the attack to hit, you expend the Psionic Energy die.

entries.name: Psychic Teleportation
entries.description: As a bonus action, you manifest one of your Psychic Blades, expend one Psionic Energy die and roll it, and throw the blade at an unoccupied space you can see, up to a number of feet away equal to 10 times the number rolled. You then teleport to that space, and the blade vanishes.

entries.name: Invisibility
entries: As an action, you can magically become invisible, along with anything you are wearing or carrying, for 1 hour or until you dismiss this effect (no action required).
This invisibility ends early immediately after you deal damage to a creature or you force a creature to make a saving throw.

items: Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest, unless you expend a {@item Psionic Energy die|tasha.resource.psionic-energy-die} to use this feature again.

entries.name: Psychic Blades (17th Level)
entries.description: When you use your Psychic Blades to deal Sneak Attack damage to a creature, you can force that target to make a Wisdom saving throw (DC equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Dexterity modifier). If the save fails, the target is stunned for 1 minute. The stunned target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

entries.name: Restriction
entries.description: Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest, unless you expend three Psionic Energy dice to use it again.

entries.name: Class Features and Subclasses
entries: The sorcerer class receives new features and sub­ classes in this section.

entries.name: Metamagic Options
entries: The 3rd-level sorcerer feature grants access to additional Metamagic options, though the specific options are not detailed in the provided text.

items: Access to extra Metamagic options beyond the standard choices
Enhanced flexibility in modifying spells during casting

entries: If you make an attack roll for a spell and miss, you can spend 2 sorcery points to reroll the d20, and you must use the new roll.
You can use Seeking Spell even if you have already used a different Metamagic option during the casting of the spell.

entries.name: Transmuted Spell
entries: When you cast a spell that deals a type of damage from the following list, you can spend 1 sorcery point to change that damage type to one of the other listed types: acid, cold, fire, lightning, poison, thunder.
The spell’s damage type is changed to another type from the list, but the spell’s other effects remain unchanged.

entries.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: Whenever you reach a level in this class that grants the Ability Score Improvement feature, you can do one of the following, representing the magic within you flowing in new ways:

entries.name: Sorcerous Origin
entries: At 1st level, a sorcerer gains the Sorcerous Origin feature, which offers you the choice of a subclass. The following options are available to you when making that choice: the Aberrant Mind and the Clockwork Soul.

entries.name: Aberrant Mind
entries: The Aberrant Mind's psychic power, from beyond the Fey's lore per... (text corrupted)

entries.name: TASNA Psionic Influence
entries: An alien influence has wrapped its tendrils around your mind, giving you psionic power. You can now touch other minds with that power and alter the world around you by using it to control the magical energy of the multiverse.

items: Replace one of the options you chose for the Metamagic feature with a different Metamagic option available to you.
Replace one cantrip you learned from this class's Spellcasting feature with another cantrip from the sorcerer spell list.

entries.content: You were exposed to the Far Realm's warping influence. You are convinced that a tentacle is now growing on you, but no one else can see it.
A psychic wind from the Astral Plane carried psionic energy to you. When you use your powers, faint motes of light sparkle around you.
You once suffered the dominating powers of an aboleth, leaving a psychic splinter in your mind.
You were implanted with a mind flayer tadpole, but the ceremorphosis never completed. And now its psionic power is yours. When you use it, your flesh shines with a strange mucus.
As a child, you had an imaginary friend that looked like a flumph or a strange platypus-like creature. One day, it gifted you with psionic powers, which have ended up being not so imaginary.
Your nightmares whisper the truth to you: your psionic powers are not your own. You draw them from your parasitic twin!

entries.name: Psionic Spells
entries: You learn additional spells when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown on the Psionic Spells table. Each of these spells counts as a sorcerer spell for you, but it doesn't count against the number of sorcerer spells you know.

entries.name: Spell Replacement
entries: Whenever you gain a sorcerer level, you can replace one spell you gained from this feature with another spell of the same level. The new spell must be a divination or an enchantment spell from the sorcerer, warlock, or wizard spell list.

entries.name: Action
entries: As a bonus action, choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of you.

entries.name: Range
entries: You and the chosen creature can speak telepathically with each other while the two of you are within a number of miles of each other equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1 mile).

entries.name: Duration
entries: The telepathic connection lasts for a number of minutes equal to your sorcerer level.

entries.name: Ending Conditions
entries: It ends early if you are incapacitated or die, or if you use this ability to form a connection with a different creature.

entries.text: The source text for 'PSI O N I C SPELLS (page 68)' appears to contain severe OCR artifacts and is incomplete. The fragment 'T1EFCING ABERRl\NT MINO' cannot be meaningfully parsed or linked to any coherent game entity. No structured data could be extracted.

entries.name: Psionic Spellcasting (6th Level)
entries.description: When you cast any spell of 1st level or higher from your Psionic Spells feature, you can cast it by spending a spell slot as normal or by spending a number of sorcery points equal to the spell's level. If you cast the spell using sorcery points, it requires no verbal or somatic components, and it requires no material components, unless they are consumed by the spell.

entries.name: Psychic Resistance
entries.description: You gain resistance to psychic damage.
You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed or frightened.

entries.name: Aberrant Mind (14th Level)
entries: As a bonus action, you can spend 1 or more sorcery points to magically transform your body for 10 minutes. For each sorcery, you gain one of the following benefits:
You can see any invisible creature within 60 feet of you, provided it isn't behind total cover. Your eyes also turn black or become writhing sensory tendrils.
You gain a flying speed equal to your walking speed, and you can hover. As you fly, your skin glistens with mucus or shines with an otherworldly light.

You gain a swimming speed equal to twice your walking speed, and you can breathe underwater. Moreover, gills grow from your neck or fan out from behind your ears, your fingers become webbed, or you grow writhing cilia that extend through your clothing.

Your body, along with any equipment you are wearing or carrying, becomes slimy and pliable. You can move through any space as narrow as 1 inch without squeezing, and you can spend 5 feet of movement to escape from nonmagical restraints or being grappled.

entries.name: Aberrant Mind (18th Level)
entries.description: As an action, you can teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within 12,000 feet. Immediately after disappearing, each creature within 30 feet of your former space must make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 3d10} force damage and is pulled toward your former space, ending in an unoccupied space as close as possible. On a success, the creature takes half damage and isn't pulled. Once used, this feature requires a long rest to refresh unless you spend 5 sorcery points to use it again.

entries.name: CLOCKWORK SOUL
entries: The cosmic force of order has suffused you with magic. That power arises from Mechanus or a realm like it—a plane of existence shaped entirely by clockwork efficiency. You, or someone from your lineage, might have become entangled in the machinations of the modrons, the orderly beings who inhabit Mechanus. Perhaps your ancestor even took part in the Great Modron March. Whatever its origin within you, the power of order can seem strange to others, but for you, it is part of a vast and glorious system.

entries.name: Spell Selection
entries: You learn additional spells when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown on the Clockwork Spells table. Each of these spells counts as a sorcerer spell for you, but it doesn't count against the number of sorcerer spells you know.

entries.name: Replacement Rules
entries: Whenever you gain a sorcerer level, you can replace one spell you gained from this feature with another spell of the same level. The new spell must be an abjuration or a transmutation spell from the sorcerer, warlock, or wizard spell list.

entries.name: Reaction Feature
entries: Your connection to the plane of absolute order allows you to equalize chaotic moments. When a creature you can see within 60 feet of you is about to roll a d20 with advantage or disadvantage, you can use your reaction to prevent the roll from being affected by advantage and disadvantage.

entries.name: Usage Limits
entries: You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Multiclassing Considerations
entries: This feature is part of the Clockwork Soul class, and its spell selection is tied to the class's progression.

entries.name: Clockwork Spells
entries.description: When casting a sorcerer spell that targets a creature, you can choose to manifest your connection to order by consulting the Manifestations of Order table. Choose or roll to determine a specific manifestation effect, which occurs as part of casting the spell.

items: 2 The hands of a clock spin in your eyes.
3 Your skin glows with a brassy sheen.
4 Floating equations and geometric objects overlay your body.
5 Your spellcasting focus temporarily takes the form of a Tiny clockwork mechanism.
6 The ticking of gears or ringing of a clock can be heard by you and those affected by your magic.

entries.name: Clockwork Soul 6th-level Feature
entries: As an action, you can expend 1 to 5 sorcery points to create a magical ward around yourself or another creature you can see within 30 feet. The ward lasts until you finish a long rest or until you use this feature again.

The ward is represented by a number of d8s equal to the number of sorcery points spent. When the warded creature takes damage, it can expend a number of those dice, roll them, and reduce the damage taken by the total rolled on those dice.

entries.name: Duration
items: Until you finish a long rest
Until you use this feature again

entries.name: Damage Reduction Mechanics
entries: The warded creature can expend dice from the ward to reduce damage. Each expended die is rolled and its result is subtracted from the damage taken.
The number of d8s in the ward equals the sorcery points spent (e.g., 3 sorcery points = 3d8).

entries.name: Clockwork Soul Feature (14th Level)
entries: You gain the ability to align your consciousness to the endless calculations of Mechanus. As a bonus action, you can enter this state for 1 minute. For the duration, attack rolls against you can't benefit from advantage, and whenever you make an attack roll, an ability check, or a saving throw, you can treat a roll of 9 or lower on the d20 as a 10.

items: Once you use this bonus action, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest, unless you spend 5 sorcery points to use it again.

entries.name: Clockwork Cavalcade
entries: You summon spirits of order to expunge disorder around you. As an action, you summon the spirits in a 30-foot cube originating from you. The spirits look like modrons or other constructs of your choice. The spirits are intangible and invulnerable, and they create the following effects within the cube before vanishing:

items: The spirits restore up to 100 hit points, divided as you choose among any number of creatures of your choice in the cube.
Any damaged objects entirely in the cube are repaired instantly.
Every spell of 6th level or lower ends on creatures and objects of your choice in the cube.

entries: Once you use this action, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest, unless you spend 7 sorcery points to use it again.

entries.name: Warlock
entries: The warlock class receives new features and subclasses in this section.

entries.name: PACT OF THE TALISMAN
entries.description: Your patron gives you an amulet, a talisman that can aid the wearer when the need is great. When the wearer fails an ability check, they can add a d4 to the roll, potentially turning the roll into a success. This benefit can be used a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and all expended uses are restored when you finish a long rest.

special_rules.name: Talisman Replacement
entries: If you lose the talisman, you can perform a 1-hour ceremony to receive a replacement from your patron. This ceremony can be performed during a short or long rest, and it destroys the previous amulet.

special_rules.name: Death Effect
entries: The talisman turns to ash when you die.

entries.name: Ability Score Improvement (4th Level)
entries: Replace one cantrip you learned from this class's Pact Magic feature with another cantrip from the warlock spell list.
Replace the option you chose for the Pact Boon feature with one of that feature's other options.
If you're 12th level or higher, replace one spell from your Mystic Arcanum feature with another warlock spell of the same level.
If this change makes you ineligible for any of your Eldritch Invocations, you must also replace them now, choosing invocations for which you qualify.

items: Replace one cantrip from Pact Magic with another warlock cantrip
Replace Mystic Arcanum spell (12th level or higher)

entries.name: Eldritch Mind
entries.description: You have advantage on Constitution saving throws that you make to maintain your concentration on a spell.

entries.name: Prerequisite
entries: 5th-level warlock, Pact of the Tome feature

entries.name: Description
entries: A new page appears in your Book of Shadows. With your permission, a creature can use its action to write its name on that page, which can contain a number of names equal to your proficiency bonus.

You can cast the sending spell, targeting a creature whose name is on the page, without using a spell slot and without using material components. To do so, you must write the message on the page. The target hears the message in their mind, and if the target replies, their message appears on the page, rather than in your mind. The writing disappears after 1 minute.
As an action, you can magically erase a name on the page by touching it.

entries.name: Prerequisites
entries: 9th-level warlock
Pact of the Tome feature

entries.name: Feature Description
entries: A new page appears in your Book of Shadows. With your permission, a creature can use its action to write its name on that page, which can contain a number of names equal to your proficiency bonus.

When any creature whose name is on the page is reduced to 0 hit points but not killed outright, the creature magically drops to 1 hit point instead. Once this magic is triggered, no creature can benefit from it until you finish a long rest.
As an action, you can magically erase a name on the page by touching it.

entries.name: Prerequisite
entries: Pact of the Chain feature

entries.name: Benefits
entries: The familiar gains either a flying speed or a swimming speed (your choice) of 40 feet.
As a bonus action, you can command the familiar to take the Attack action.
The familiar's weapon attacks are considered magical for the purpose of overcoming immunity and resistance to nonmagical attacks.
If the familiar forces a creature to make a saving throw, it uses your spell save DC.
When the familiar takes damage, you can use your reaction to grant it resistance against that damage.

entries.name: Flavor Text
entries: A Tie Flaming Warlock Calls on the Power of His Talisman.

entries.name: Protection of the Talisman
entries.description: When the wearer of your talisman fails a saving throw, they can add a d4 to the roll, potentially turning the save into a success. This benefit can be used a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and all expended uses are restored when you finish a long rest.
entries.prerequisites: 7th-level warlock, Pact of the Talisman feature

entries.name: Rebuke of the Talisman
entries.description: When the wearer of your talisman is hit by an attacker you can see within 30 feet of you, you can use your reaction to deal psychic damage equal to your proficiency bonus to the attacker and push it up to 10 feet away from the talisman's wearer.

entries.name: Undying Servitude
entries.description: You can cast animate dead without using a spell slot. Once you do so, you can't cast it in this way again until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Otherworldly Patron
entries: At 1st level, a warlock gains the Otherworldly Patron feature, which offers you the choice of a subclass.

entries.name: Patron Description
entries: You have plunged into a pact with the deeps. An entity of the ocean, the Elemental Plane of Water, or another otherworldly sea now allows you to draw on its thalassic power. Is it merely using you to learn about terrestrial realms, or does it want you to open cosmic floodgates and drown the world?

Perhaps you were born into a generational cult that venerates the Fathomless and its spawn. Or you might have been shipwrecked and on the brink of drowning when your patron's grasp offered you a chance at life. Whatever the reason for your pact, the sea and its unknown depths call to you.

items: Godlike hallucinations dreamed into being by kuo-toa
entries.name: Possible Patrons
Fathomless Feature
entries: The Fathomless lets you choose from an expanded list of spells when you learn a warlock spell. The following spells are added to the warlock spell list for you.

entries.name: Spectral Tentacle
entries: As a bonus action, you create a 10-foot-long tentacle at a point you can see within 60 feet of you. The tentacle lasts for 1 minute or until you use this feature to create another tentacle.

When you create the tentacle, you can make a melee spell attack against one creature within 10 feet of it. On a hit, the target takes 1d8 cold damage, and its speed is reduced by 10 feet until the start of your next turn. When you reach 10th level in this class, the damage increases to 2d8.
As a bonus action on your turn, you can move the tentacle up to 30 feet and repeat the attack.

items: You can summon the tentacle a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Swimming Ability
entries: You gain a swimming speed of 40 feet, and you can breathe underwater.

entries.name: OCEANIC SOUL
entries.description: You are now even more at home in the depths. You gain {@resistance cold}. In addition, when you are fully submerged, any creature that is also fully submerged can understand your speech, and you can understand theirs.

entries.name: Tentacle of the Deeps
entries: When you or a creature you can see takes damage while within 10 feet of the tentacle, you can use your reaction to choose one of those creatures and reduce the damage to that creature by {@damage 1d8}.
At 10th level, the damage reduced by the tentacle increases to {@damage 2d8}.

entries.name: Spell Learned
entries: You learn the spell {@item Evard's black tentacles|phb14.spell.Evard's_black_tentacles}. It counts as a warlock spell for you, but it doesn't count against the number of spells you know.

entries.name: Cast Without Spell Slot
entries: You can also cast {@item Evard's black tentacles|phb14.spell.Evard's_black_tentacles} once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Temporary Hit Points
entries: Whenever you cast this spell, your patron's magic bolsters you, granting you a number of temporary hit points equal to your warlock level.

entries.name: Concentration Immunity
entries: Moreover, damage can't break your concentration on this spell.

entries.name: 14th-level Fathomless Feature
entries.description: You can magically open temporary conduits to watery destinations. As an action, you can teleport yourself and up to five other willing creatures that you can see within 30 feet of you. Amid a whirl of tentacles, you all vanish and then reappear up to 1 mile away in a body of water you've seen (pond size or larger) or within 30 feet of it, each of you appearing in an unoccupied space within 30 feet of the others.

entries.name: Teleportation Details
items: Range: Up to 1 mile from a seen body of water (pond size or larger) or within 30 feet of it
Number of Creatures: Up to five willing creatures (including self) within 30 feet
Appearance: Unoccupied spaces within 30 feet of each other

entries.name: Cooldown
entries.description: Once used, this feature cannot be used again until after a short or long rest.

entries.name: Patron Selection
entries: You choose your patron's kind or determine it randomly using the {@item Genie Kind table|tasha.table.genie-kind}.
Noble genies rule vast fiefs on the Elemental Planes and wield power rivaling lesser deities. They delight in turning the table on mortals who bind them into servitude.

entries.name: Patron Kind
entries: Your patron is a noble genie with motivations that vary but often include arrogance and a desire to expand their influence.

entries.name: Genie Expanded Spells
entries: The Genie feature allows you to choose from an expanded list of spells when you learn a warlock spell.

items: Genie spells are added to the warlock spell list
Spells are associated with your patron's kind: dao, djinni, efreeti, or marid

entries.text: See the Genie Expanded Spells table for specific spell additions.

entries.name: Genie's Vessel
entries: Your patron gifts you a magical vessel that grants you a measure of the genie's power. The vessel is a Tiny object, and you can use it as a spellcasting focus for your warlock spells.
You decide what the object is, or you can determine what it is randomly by rolling on the Genie's Vessel table.

entries.name: Bottled Respite
entries: As an action, you can magically vanish and enter your vessel, which remains in the space you left. The interior of the vessel is an extradimensional space in the shape of a 20-foot-radius cylinder, 20 feet high, and resembles your vessel. The interior is appointed with cushions and low tables and is a comfortable temperature. While inside, you can hear the area around your vessel as if you were in its space.

You can remain inside the vessel up to a number of hours equal to twice your proficiency bonus. You exit the vessel early if you use a bonus action to leave, if you die, or if the vessel is destroyed.

When you exit the vessel, you appear in the unoccupied space closest to it. Any objects left in the vessel remain there until carried out, and if the vessel is destroyed, every object stored there harmlessly appears in the unoccupied spaces closest to the vessel's former space.
Once you enter the, you can't enter again until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Genie's Wrath
entries: Once during each of your turns when you hit with an attack roll, you can deal extra damage to the target equal to your proficiency bonus. The type of this damage is determined by your patron: {@damage bludgeoning} (dao), {@damage thunder} (djinni), {@damage fire} (efreeti), or {@damage cold} (marid).

entries.name: Vessel Statistics
entries: The vessel's AC equals your spell save DC. Its hit points equal your warlock level plus your proficiency bonus, and it is immune to poison and psychic damage.

entries.name: Replacement Ceremony
entries: If the vessel is destroyed or you lose it, you can perform a 1-hour ceremony to receive a replacement from your patron. This ceremony can be performed during a short or long rest, and the previous vessel is destroyed if it still exists. The vessel vanishes in a flare of elemental power when you die.

entries.name: Damage Resistance
entries: You gain resistance to one damage type determined by your patron's kind: bludgeoning (dao), thunder (djinni), fire (efreeti), or cold (marid).

entries.name: Flying Speed (Bonus Action)
entries: As a bonus action, you can grant yourself a flying speed of 30 feet that lasts for 10 minutes. During this time, you can hover.
You can use this ability a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. When you finish a long rest, you regain all expended uses.

entries.name: Genie's Vessel (10th Level)
entries: When you enter your Genie's Vessel via the Bottled Respite feature, you can now choose up to five willing creatures that you can see within 30 feet of you, and the chosen creatures are drawn into the vessel with you.

entries.name: Ejection Mechanics
items: As a bonus action, you can eject any number of creatures from the vessel.
Everyone is ejected if you leave or die, or if the vessel is destroyed.

entries.name: Rest Benefit
entries: Anyone (including you) who remains within the vessel for at least 10 minutes gains the benefit of finishing a short rest.
Anyone can add your proficiency bonus to the number of hit points they regain if they spend any Hit Dice as part of a short rest there.

entries.name: 14th-Level Genie Feature
entries: As an action, you can speak to your Genie's Vessel to cast a spell of 6th level or lower with a casting time of 1 action. The spell can be from any class's spell list, and you don't need to meet its requirements (including costly components) for it to take effect.
Once used, this feature cannot be used again until you complete ld4 long rests.

items: The spellcasting ability used for this feature is determined by your Genie's class.

entries.name: Wizard Class Features
entries.description: The wizard class receives new features and subclasses in this section.

entries.name: Cantrip Formulas
entries: At 3rd level, you can replace one wizard cantrip you know with another cantrip from the wizard spell list whenever you finish a long rest and consult the arcane formulas you've scribed in your spellbook.

entries.name: Arcane Tradition
entries: At 2nd level, a wizard gains the Arcane Tradition feature, which offers you the choice of a subclass.
The following options are available to you when making that choice: Bladesinging and the Order of Scribes.

entries.name: Bladesinging
entries.content: Bladesingers master a tradition of wizardry that incorporates swordplay and dance. Originally created by elves, this tradition has been adopted by non-elf practitioners, who honor and expand on the elven ways. In combat, a bladesinger uses a series of intricate, elegant maneuvers that fend off harm and allow the bladesinger to channel magic into devastating attacks and a cunning defense. Many who have observed a bladesinger at work remember the display as one of the more beautiful experiences in their life, a glorious dance accompanied by a singing blade.

entries.name: Training in War and Song
entries: You gain proficiency with light armor, and you gain proficiency with one type of one-handed melee weapon of your choice.
You also gain proficiency in the Performance skill if you don't already have it.

items: Proficiency with one type of one-handed melee weapon of your choice
Proficiency in the Performance skill (if not already possessed)

entries.name: Bladesong
entries: You can invoke an elven magic called the Bladesong as a bonus action, provided you aren't wearing medium or heavy armor or using a shield. The Bladesong lasts 1 minute and ends if you are incapacitated, wear armor/shield, or use two hands to attack. You can dismiss it at any time.
While active, you gain the following benefits:

items: AC bonus equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum +1)
Bonus to Constitution saving throws to maintain concentration on spells (equals Intelligence modifier, minimum +1)

entries: You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. All uses are regained after a long rest.

entries.name: Multiclassing Considerations
entries: This feature is exclusive to the Bladesinger subclass. See {@item Bladesinger|tasha.class.bladesinger} for full subclass details.

entries.name: Cantrip Substitution
entries: Moreover, you can cast one of your cantrips in place of one of those attacks.

entries.name: Bladesinging Feature (10th Level)
entries.description: While your Bladesong is active, you can use your reaction when taking damage to expend one spell slot. Reduce the damage to you by an amount equal to five times the spell slot's level. {@damage 5×spell slot level} This effect triggers once per damage instance.

entries.name: Bladesinging (14th Level)
entries.description: While your Bladesong is active, you can add your Intelligence modifier (minimum of +1) to the damage of your melee weapon attacks.

items: Add your Intelligence modifier (minimum of +1) to melee weapon attack damage

entries.name: Magical Quill
entries: As a bonus action, you can magically create a Tiny quill in your free hand. The magic quill has the following properties:
The quill doesn't require ink. When you write with it, it produces ink in a color of your choice on the writing surface.
The time you must spend to copy a spell into your spellbook equals 2 minutes per spell level if you use the quill for the transcription.
You can erase anything you write with the quill if you wave the feather over the text as a bonus action, provided the text is within 5 feet of you.
This quill disappears if you create another one or if you die.

entries.name: Arcane Sentience in Spellbook
entries: Using specially prepared inks and ancient incantations passed down by your wizardly order, you have awakened an arcane sentience within your spellbook.
While you are holding the book, it grants you the following benefits:

children.name: Spellcasting Focus
entries: You can use the book as a spellcasting focus for your wizard spells.

children.name: Damage Type Replacement
entries: When you cast a wizard spell with a spell slot, you can temporarily replace its damage type with a type that appears in another spell in your spellbook, which magically alters the spell's formula for this casting only. The latter spell must be of the same level as the spell slot you expend.

children.name: Ritual Casting Time
entries: When you cast a wizard spell as a ritual, you can use the spell's normal casting time, rather than adding 10 minutes to it. Once you use this benefit, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

children.name: Replacing the Spellbook
entries: If necessary, you can replace the book over the course of a short rest by using your Wizardly Quill to write arcane sigils in a blank book or a magic spell book to which you're attuned. At the end of the rest, your spellbook's consciousness is summoned into the new book, which the consciousness transforms into your spellbook, along with all its spells. If the previous book still existed somewhere, all the spells vanish from its pages.

entries.name: Conjuring the Spectral Mind
entries: As a bonus action while the Awakened Spellbook is on your person, you can cause its mind to manifest as a Tiny spectral object within 60 feet of you. The spectral mind is intangible, doesn't occupy space, and sheds dim light in a 10-foot radius. It appears as a ghostly tome, cascade of text, or scholar from the past (your choice).

entries.name: Spectral Mind Abilities
entries: While manifested, the spectral mind has darkvision with a 60-foot range and can hear and see. It telepathically shares sensory input with you (no action required).

When casting a wizard spell on your turn, you can cast it as if in the spectral mind's space, using its senses. You can do this a number of times per day equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining uses on a long rest.

entries.name: Moving the Spectral Mind
entries: As a bonus action, you can move the spectral mind up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you or it can see. It passes through creatures but not objects.

entries.name: Ending the Manifestation
entries: The spectral mind stops manifesting if: it is more than 300 feet from you, dispel magic is cast on it, the Awakened Spellbook is destroyed, you die, or you dismiss it as a bonus action.

entries.name: Cooldown and Spell Slot Use
entries: You cannot conjure the mind again until after a long rest, unless you expend a spell slot of any level to conjure it again.

entries.name: Magic Scroll Creation
entries.description: Whenever you finish a long rest, you can create one magic scroll by touching your Wizardly Quill to a blank piece of paper or parchment. The spellbook must be within 5 feet of you when making the scroll.

entries.name: Spell Requirements
entries.description: The chosen spell must be of 1st or 2nd level and have a casting time of 1 action.

entries.name: Enhanced Spell
entries.description: The spell's power is enhanced, counting as one level higher than normal when copied onto the scroll.

entries.name: Casting the Scroll
entries.description: You can cast the spell from the scroll by reading it as an action. The scroll is unintelligible to others and vanishes when cast or after finishing your next long rest.

entries.name: Scroll Crafting Cost
entries.description: Crafting spell scrolls costs half the gold and time required, if using your Wizardly Quill.

entries.name: Awakened Spellbook's Mind
entries: While the spellbook is on your person, you have advantage on all Intelligence (Arcana) checks, as the spellbook helps you remember magical lore.

items: When taking damage while your spellbook's mind is manifested, you can use your reaction to dismiss the spectral mind, using its magic to save yourself.

Roll 3d6. The spellbook temporarily loses spells of your choice with a combined spell level equal to or higher than the roll (e.g., a total of 9 could mean one 9th-level spell, three 3rd-level spells, etc.).
If there aren't enough spells to cover this cost, you drop to 0 hit points.

entries: Until you finish ld6 long rests, you cannot cast the lost spells, even if found on a scroll or in another spellbook. After completing the required rests, the spells reappear.
Once used, this reaction cannot be used again until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: New Spells Availability
entries: This section contains new spells that the DM may add to a campaign, making them available to player character and monster spellcasters alike.

The Spells table lists the new spells, ordering them by level. The table also notes the school of magic of a spell, whether it requires concentration, whether it bears the ritual tag, and which classes have access to it.
If you'd like to use any of these spells, talk to your DM, who may allow some, all, or none of them.

entries.name: Blade of Disaster
description: You create a blade-shaped planar rift about 3 feet long in an unoccupied space you can see within range. The blade lasts for the duration.

items: When you cast this spell, you can make up to two melee spell attacks with the blade, each one against a creature, loose object, or structure within 5 feet of the blade.
On a hit, the target takes {@damage 4d12} force damage.
This attack scores a critical hit if the number on the d20 is 18 or higher.
On a critical hit, the blade deals an extra {@damage 8d12} force damage (for a total of {@damage 12d12} force damage).

description.name: Bonus Action Use
entries: As a bonus action on your turn, you can move the blade up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see and then make up to two melee spell attacks with it again.

description.name: Passing Through Barriers
entries: The blade can harmlessly pass through any barrier, including a wall of force.

entries.name: BOOMING BLADE

components.m: a melee weapon worth at least 1 sp

entries.description: You brandish the weapon used in the spell's casting and make a melee attack with it against one creature within 5 feet of you. On a hit, the target suffers the weapon attack's normal effects and then becomes sheathed in booming energy until the start of your next turn. If the target willingly moves 5 feet or more before then, the target takes {@damage 1d8} thunder damage, and the spell ends. This spell's damage increases when you reach certain levels. At 5th level, the melee attack deals an extra {@damage 1d8} thunder damage to the target on a hit, and the damage the target takes for moving increases to {@damage 2d8}. Both damage rolls increase by {@damage 1d8} at 11th level (2d8 and 3d8) and again at 17th level (3d8 and 4d8).

components.m: a magic item or a willing creature from the destination world

entities.name: Traveli Ng to Oth Er Worlds

entries.name: Traveling to Other Worlds
entries: The Material Plane contains an infinite number of worlds, including well-documented ones like Oerth, Tori!, Krynn, and Eberron. Some worlds are reflections or distortions of the First World, a primordial state shattered by a cataclysm to create the multiverse.

items: Great Journey: A perilous voyage aboard a magical vessel.
Dream of Other Worlds: Travelers fall into a deep slumber and dream themselves into a new realm (spell: *Dream of the Blue Veil*).
Leap to Another Realm: A spellcaster uses *teleportation circle* or *teleport* to appear in a known location on another world.

entries.name: Spell: Dream of the Blue Veil
entries: Casting this spell requires a magic item from the target world or a creature born on that world. The destination is a safe location within 1 mile of the item's origin or the creature's birthplace.

You and up to eight willing creatures within range fall unconscious for 1 minute, experiencing visions of a world (e.g., Oerth, Tori!, Krynn, Eberron). If the spell lasts its full duration, each traveler pulls back a mysterious blue curtain and is mentally/physically transported to the world in the visions.
The spell ends early on a creature if it takes damage. If the caster takes damage, the spell ends for all, with no transportation.

entries.name: Green-Flame Blade

components.m: a melee weapon worth at least 1 sp

entries.description:
You brandish the weapon used in the spell's casting and make a melee attack with it against one creature within 5 feet of you. On a hit, the target suffers the weapon attack's normal effects, and you can cause green fire to leap from the target to a different creature of your choice that you can see within 5 feet of it. The second creature takes fire damage equal to your spellcasting ability modifier.

{@damage 1d8} fire damage to the first target increases at 5th level, and the fire damage to the second creature increases to {@damage 1d8} + your spellcasting ability modifier. Both damage rolls increase by {@damage 1d8} at 11th level (totaling {@damage 2d8} and {@damage 2d8}) and 17th level (totaling {@damage 3d8} and {@damage 3d8}).

entries.name: Psychic Resistance
entries.description: For the duration, you or one willing creature you can see within range has resistance to psychic damage, as well as advantage on Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws.
entries.higher_level: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, you can target one additional creature for each slot level above 3rd. The creatures must be within 30 feet of each other when you target them.

entries.name: LIGHTNING LURE
entries.description: You create a lash of lightning energy that strikes at one creature of your choice that you can see within 15 feet of you. The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pulled up to 10 feet in a straight line toward you and then take 1d8 lightning damage if it is within 5 feet of you.

entries.name: Summon Aberration

components.m: a pickled tentacle and an eyeball in a platinum-inlaid vial worth at least 400 gp

entries.description: You call forth an aberrant spirit. It manifests in an unoccupied space you can see within range. This corporeal form uses the Aberrant Spirit stat block. When you cast the spell, choose Beholderkin, Slaad, or Star Spawn. The creature resembles an aberration of that kind, which determines certain traits in its stat block. The creature disappears when it drops to 0 hit points or when the spell ends. The creature is an ally to you and your companions. In combat, it shares your initiative count but takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you). If you don't issue any, it takes the Dodge action and uses its move to avoid danger.

entries.text: The source text describes a monster (aberration) named 'Aberrant Spirit' rather than a spell. No spell data was found for this entity.

entries.name: Summon Beast

components.m: a feather, tuft of fur, and fish tail inside a gilded acorn worth at least 200 gp

entries.description: You call forth a bestial spirit. It manifests in an unoccupied space you can see within range. This corporeal form uses the Bestial Spirit stat block. When you cast the spell, choose an environment: Air, Land, or Water. The creature resembles an animal native to the chosen environment, which determines certain traits in its stat block. The creature disappears when it drops to 0 hit points or when the spell ends. The creature is an ally to you and your companions. In combat, it shares your initiative count but takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you). If you don't issue any, it takes the Dodge action and uses its move to avoid danger.

entries.name: Bestial Spirit
entries: A 3rd-level transmutation spell that conjures a beast with the statistics provided below. The beast takes the form of a chosen creature, such as a dire wolf, grizzly bear, or other beast. The caster can choose the form of the beast, which must be a creature with the beast type. The beast appears in an unoccupied space within 60 feet of the caster. The spell lasts for 1 minute, and the beast can be dismissed as a bonus action. The beast is under the caster's control and follows their commands. If the caster is incapacitated, the beast is no longer under their control and acts on its own accord. The beast can be dismissed at will, and if dismissed, it vanishes without a trace.

entries.name: Summon Celestial

components.m: a golden reliquary worth at least 500 gp

entries.description: You call forth a celestial spirit. It manifests in an angelic form in an unoccupied space you can see within range. This corporeal form uses the Celestial Spirit stat block. When you cast the spell, choose Avenger or Defender. Your choice determines the creature's attack in its stat block. The creature disappears when it drops to 0 hit points or when the spell ends. The creature is an ally to you and your companions. In combat, it shares your initiative count but takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you). If you don't issue any, it takes the Dodge action and uses its move to avoid danger.
entries.higher_level: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, use the higher level wherever the spell's level appears in the stat block.

entries.name: Summon Construct

components.m: an ornate stone and metal lockbox worth at least 400 gp

entries.description: You call forth the spirit of a construct. It manifests in an unoccupied space you can see within range. This corporeal form uses the Construct Spirit stat block. When you cast the spell, choose a material: Clay, Metal, or Stone. The creature resembles a golem or a modron (your choice) made of the chosen material, which determines certain traits in its stat block. The creature disappears when it drops to 0 hit points or when the spell ends. The creature is an ally to you and your companions. In combat, it shares your initiative count but takes its turn immediately after yours.

entries.name: Construct Spirit
entries: This entry appears to describe a creature's statistics rather than a spell. The text provides details about a Medium construct with the following attributes:
Armor Class: 13 + the level of the spell (natural armor)
Hit Points: 40 + 15 for each spell level above 3rd
Speed: 30 ft.
Ability Scores: STR 18 (+4), DEX 10 (+0), CON 18 (+4)

entries.name: Summon Elemental

components.m: air, a pebble, ash, and water inside a gold-inlaid vial worth at least 400 gp

entries.description: You call forth an elemental spirit. It manifests in an unoccupied space you can see within range. This corporeal form uses the Elemental Spirit stat block. When you cast the spell, choose an element: Air, Earth, Fire, or Water. The creature resembles a bipedal form wreathed in the chosen element, which determines certain traits in its stat block. The creature disappears when it drops to 0 hit points or when the spell ends. The creature is an ally to you and your companions. In combat, it shares your initiative count but takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you). If you don't issue any, it takes the Dodge action and uses its move to avoid danger.

entries.name: Elemental Spirit
entries: Medium elemental
Armor Class 11  + the level of  the spell  (natural armor)
Hit Points 50 + 10 for each spell level above 4th
Speed 40 ft.; burrow 40 ft.  (Earth only); fly 40 ft.  (hover)  (Air only); swim 40 ft. (Water only)
Damage Resistances acid  (Water only); lightning and thunder (Air only); piercing and slashing (Earth only)
Damage Immunities poison; fire (Fire only)
Condition Immunities exhaustion, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, unconscious
Senses darkvision 60 ft., passive Perception 10
Languages Primordial, understands the languages you speak
Challenge -
Proficiency Bonus equals your bonus
Amorphous Form (Air, Fire, and Water Only). The elemental can move through a space as narrow as 1  inch wide without squeezing.

entries.name: Summon Fey

components.m: a gilded flower worth at least 300 gp

description: You call forth a fey spirit. It manifests in an unoccupied space that you can see within range. This corporeal form uses the Fey Spirit stat block. When you cast the spell, choose a mood: Fuming, Mirthful, or Tricksy. The creature resembles a fey creature of your choice marked by the chosen mood, which determines one of the traits in its stat block. The creature disappears when it drops to 0 hit points or when the spell ends.

The creature is an ally to you and your companions. In combat, the creature shares your initiative count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you). If you don't issue any, it takes the Dodge action and uses its move to avoid danger.

entries.name: Fey Spirit
entries: Small fey
Armor Class 12 + the level of the spell (natural armor)
Hit Points 30 + 0 for each spell level above 3rd
Speed 40 ft.
Condition Immunities charmed
Senses darkvision 60 ft., passive Perception 10
Languages Sylvan, understands the languages you speak
Challenge
Proficiency Bonus equals your bonus
ACTIONS
Multiattack. The fey makes a number of attacks equal to half this spell's level (rounded down).
Shortsword. Melee Weapon Attack: + your spell attack modifier to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 1d6 + 3 + the spell's level piercing damage + 1d6 force damage.
BONUS ACTIONS
Fey Step. The fey magically teleports up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space it can see. Then one of the following effects occurs, based on the fey's chosen mood:
Fuming. The fey has advantage on the next attack roll it makes before the end of this turn.

Mirthful. The fey can force one creature it can see within 10 feet of it to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. Unless the save succeeds, the target is charmed by you and the fey for 1 minute or until the target takes any damage.
Tricksy. The fey can fill a 5-foot cube within 5 feet of it with magical darkness, which lasts until the end of its next turn.
At Higher Levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, use the higher level wherever the spell's level appears in the stat block.

entries.name: Summon Fiend

components.m: humanoid blood inside a ruby vial worth at least 600 gp

description: You call forth a fiendish spirit. It manifests in an unoccupied space that you can see within range. This corporeal form uses the Fiendish Spirit stat block. When you cast the spell, choose Demon, Devil, or Yugoloth. The creature resembles a fiend of the chosen type, which determines certain traits in its stat block. The creature disappears when it drops to 0 hit points or when the spell ends.

The creature is an ally to you and your companions. In combat, the creature shares your initiative count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you). If you don't issue any, it takes the Dodge action and uses its move to avoid danger.
higher_level: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 7th level or higher, use the higher level wherever the spell's level appears in the stat block.

entries.name: Fiendish Spirit
hit_points: 50 (Demon only) or 40 (Devil only) or 60 (Yugoloth only) + 10 for each spell level above 6th

speed.value: 40 ft.; climb 40 ft. (Demon only); fly 60 ft. (Devil only)

special_abilities.name: Death Throes (Demon Only)
entries: When the fiend drops to 0 hit points or the spell ends, the fiend explodes, and each creature within 10 feet of it must make a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC. A creature takes 2d6 + this spell's level fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

special_abilities.name: Devil's Sight (Devil Only)
entries: The fiend's darkvision.

special_abilities.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The fiend has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

actions.name: Multiattack
entries: The fiend makes a number of attacks equal to half this spell's level (rounded down).

entries.name: Claws
entries.extra: Immediately after the attack hits or misses, the fiend can magically teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space it can see.

entries.name: Hurl Flame
entries.extra: If the target is a flammable object that isn't being worn or carried, it also catches fire.

components.m: tears inside a gem worth at least 300 gp

lore.name: Creature Description
entries: You call forth a shadowy spirit. It manifests in an unoccupied space that you can see within range. This corporeal form uses the Shadow Spirit stat block.
When you cast the spell, choose an emotion: Fury, Despair, or Fear. The creature resembles a misshapen biped marked by the chosen emotion, which determines certain traits in its stat block.
The creature disappears when it drops to 0 hit points or when the spell ends.

lore.name: Alliance and Combat
entries: The creature is an ally to you and your companions. In combat, the creature shares your initiative.

entries.text: The source text describes a monster (Medium monstrosity) with statistics and abilities, not a spell. No spell data was found for 'SHADOW SPIRIT'.

components.m: a gilded skull worth at least 300 gp

entries.text: You call forth an undead spirit. It manifests in an unoccupied space that you can see within range. This corporeal form uses the Undead Spirit stat block.

items: When you cast the spell, choose the creature's form: Ghostly, Putrid, or Skeletal.
The spirit resembles an undead creature with the chosen form, which determines certain traits in its stat block.

entries.text: The creature disappears when it drops to 0 hit points or when the spell ends.

entries.name: Undead Spirit
entries.description:
This spell creates a spectral undead creature with variant forms. The spirit's type and abilities depend on the variant chosen:

- **Ghostly**: A translucent, incorporeal undead with flight and the ability to pass through objects.
- **Putrid**: A decaying, flesh-like undead with a poisonous aura.

The spirit's statistics are as follows: Armor Class 11 + spell level (natural armor), HP varies by variant, and it moves at 30 ft. (fly 40 ft. hover) or 30 ft. (Ghostly only). It is immune to necrotic and poison damage and resistant to condition effects like exhaustion, frightened, paralyzed, and poisoned.

subroutines.name: Festering Aura (Putrid Only)
entries: Any creature, other than you, that starts its turn within 5 feet of the spirit must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC or be poisoned until the start of its next turn.

subroutines.name: Incorporeal Passage (Ghostly Only)
entries: The spirit can move through other creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain. If it ends its turn inside an object, it is shunted to the nearest unoccupied space and takes 1d10 force damage for every 5 feet traveled.

entries.name: Sword Burst
entries.description: You create a momentary circle of spectral blades that sweep around you. All other creatures within 5 feet of you must each succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take 1d6 force damage. This spell's damage increases by 1d6 when you reach 5th level (2d6), 11th level (3d6), and 17th level (4d6).

entries.name: Spell Description
entries: A stream of acid emanates from you in a line 30 feet long and 5 feet wide in a direction you choose. Each creature in the line must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or be covered in acid for the spell's duration or until a creature uses its action to scrape or wash the acid off itself or another creature.
A creature covered in the acid takes {@damage 2d4} acid damage at the start of each of its turns.

entries.name: At Higher Levels
items: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the damage increases by {@damage 2d4} for each slot level above 1st.

entries.name: Mechanics
entries: Casting Time: {@time 1 action}
Range: {@range line} (30 feet)
Components: V, S, M (a bit of rotten food)
Duration: {@duration timed} (up to 1 minute, concentration required)

entries.name: Tasha's Mind Whip
entries.description: You psychically lash out at one creature you can see within range. The target must make an Intelligence saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 3d6} psychic damage, and it can't take a reaction until the end of its next turn. Moreover, on its next turn, it must choose whether it gets a move, an action, or a bonus action; it gets only one of the three. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage and suffers none of the spell's other effects.

at_higher_levels.additional_creatures: one additional creature for each slot level above 2nd

entries.name: Tasha's Otherworldly Guise

components.m: an object engraved with a symbol of the Outer Planes, worth at least 500 gp

entries: You are immune to fire and poison damage (Lower Planes) or radiant and necrotic damage (Upper Planes).
You are immune to the poisoned condition (Lower Planes) or the charmed condition (Upper Planes).
Spectral wings appear on your back, giving you a flying speed of 40 feet.
You have a +2 bonus to AC.
All your weapon attacks are magical, and when you make a weapon attack, you can use your spellcasting ability modifier, instead of Strength or Dexterity, for the attack and damage rolls.

You can attack twice, instead of once, when you take the Attack action on your turn. You ignore this benefit if you already have a feature, like Extra Attack, that lets you attack more than once when you take the Attack action on your turn.

entries.name: Magic Items Overview
entries: This section introduces magic items for any campaign, including artifacts and spellcasting focuses for all classes.
A Magic Items table lists all items in the chapter, noting rarity and attunement requirements.
Items use the magic items rules from the Dungeon Master's Guide.

entries.name: Magic Tattoos
entries: A new type of wondrous item: magic tattoos. These are described as special, with examples like the robe tattoos covering the armpits and lower back, granting abilities to those who are not yet apprentices.
Tattoos are considered magical and may have unique properties tied to their placement and the wearer's abilities.

entries.name: Description
entries: Blending magic and artistry with ink and needles, magic tattoos imbue their bearers with wondrous abilities. Magic tattoos are initially bound to magic needles, which transfer their magic to a creature.

Once inscribed on a creature's body, damage or injury doesn't impair the tattoo's function, even if the tattoo is defaced. When applying a magic tattoo, a creature can customize the tattoo's appearance.
A magic tattoo can look like a brand, scarification, a birthmark, patterns of scale, or any other cosmetic alteration.

entries.name: Coverage Guidelines
entries: The rarer a magic tattoo is, the more space it typically occupies on a creature's skin. The Magic Tattoo Coverage table offers guidelines for how large a given tattoo is.

entries.name: Absorbing Tattoo
entries.description: Produced by a special needle, this magic tattoo features designs that emphasize one color. Tattoo Attunement: To attune to this item, you hold the needle to your skin where you want the tattoo to appear, pressing the needle there throughout the attunement process. When the attunement is complete, the needle turns into the ink that becomes the tattoo, which appears on the skin. If your attunement to the tattoo ends, the tattoo vanishes, and the needle reappears in your space.

features.name: Damage Resistance
entries: While the tattoo is on your skin, you have resistance to a type of damage associated with that color, as shown on the table below. The DM chooses the color or determines it randomly.

features.name: Damage Absorption
entries: When you take damage of the chosen type, you can use your reaction to gain immunity against that instance of the damage, and you regain a number of hit points equal to half the damage you would have taken. Once this reaction is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
